I've uploaded a code repository in the directory . Consider the following PR description:

<pr_description>
Add IDialogService.ShowAsync<T>(DialogParameters<T> parameters) method
### Describe your idea

Most of the time I'm going to use a dialog, I don't need a custom title. Many times I end up specifying `string.Empty` in the first argument, because the `DialogParameters` are right after it, and it's quite annoying. I had to create an ExtensionMethod to skip that annoying step, but it would be great if IDialogService already provided that in its signature.

This is the ExtensionMethod I am currently using:
```
public static class IDialogServiceMethods
{
    public static async Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<T>(this IDialogService dialogService, DialogParameters<T> parameters) where T : IComponent
    {
        return await dialogService.ShowAsync<T>("", parameters);
    }
}
```


### Example code and screenshots

Currently I would have to specify, without help from my ExtensionMethod:
`await dialogService.ShowAsync<MyDialog>("", new DialogParameters<MyDialog>() {  { x => x.MyParam, "value" } });`

You could create a new method signature to be able to use `IDialogService` as follows:
`await dialogService.ShowAsync<MyDialog>(new DialogParameters<MyDialog>() {  { x => x.MyParam, "value" } });`

</pr_description>

     1	﻿// Copyright (c) 2019 Blazored (https://github.com/Blazored)
     2	// See https://github.com/Blazored
     3	// License: MIT
     4	// Copyright (c) 2020 Adapted by MudBlazor
     5	
     6	using System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis;
     7	using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components;
     8	
     9	namespace MudBlazor
    10	{
    11	    /// <summary>
    12	    /// Service used to create, show, and close MudBlazor dialogs.
    13	    /// </summary>
    14	    /// <remarks>
    15	    /// Register this service and include a <see cref="MudDialogProvider"/> in your layout so dialogs can render and respond to close requests.
    16	    /// </remarks>
    17	    /// <seealso cref="MudDialog"/>
    18	    /// <seealso cref="MudDialogContainer"/>
    19	    /// <seealso cref="MudDialogProvider"/>
    20	    /// <seealso cref="DialogOptions"/>
    21	    /// <seealso cref="DialogParameters{T}"/>
    22	    /// <seealso cref="DialogReference"/>
    23	    public class DialogService : IDialogService
    24	    {
    25	        /// <summary>
    26	        /// Internal wrapper component that prevents overwriting parameters on existing dialog instances.
    27	        /// </summary>
    28	        /// <remarks>
    29	        /// This keeps dialog content stable while the parent fragment re-renders.
    30	        /// See: https://github.com/MudBlazor/MudBlazor/issues/10659#issuecomment-2602911059
    31	        /// </remarks>
    32	        private sealed class DialogHelperComponent : IComponent
    33	        {
    34	            private const string ChildContent = nameof(ChildContent);
    35	            private RenderFragment? _renderFragment;
    36	            private RenderHandle _renderHandle;
    37	            void IComponent.Attach(RenderHandle renderHandle) => _renderHandle = renderHandle;
    38	
    39	            Task IComponent.SetParametersAsync(ParameterView parameters)
    40	            {
    41	                if (_renderFragment is null && parameters.TryGetValue<RenderFragment>(ChildContent, out var renderFragment))
    42	                {
    43	                    _renderFragment = renderFragment;
    44	                    _renderHandle.Render(_renderFragment);
    45	                }
    46	
    47	                return Task.CompletedTask;
    48	            }
    49	
    50	            public static RenderFragment Wrap(RenderFragment renderFragment)
    51	                => builder =>
    52	                {
    53	                    builder.OpenComponent<DialogHelperComponent>(1);
    54	                    builder.AddAttribute(2, ChildContent, renderFragment);
    55	                    builder.CloseComponent();
    56	                };
    57	        }
    58	
    59	        /// <inheritdoc />
    60	        public event Func<IDialogReference, Task>? DialogInstanceAddedAsync;
    61	
    62	        /// <inheritdoc />
    63	        public event Action<IDialogReference, DialogResult?>? OnDialogCloseRequested;
    64	
    65	        /// <inheritdoc />
    66	        public Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] T>() where T : IComponent
    67	        {
    68	            return ShowAsync<T>(string.Empty, DialogParameters.Default, DialogOptions.Default);
    69	        }
    70	
    71	        /// <inheritdoc />
    72	        public Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] T>(string? title) where T : IComponent
    73	        {
    74	            return ShowAsync<T>(title, DialogParameters.Default, DialogOptions.Default);
    75	        }
    76	
    77	        /// <inheritdoc />
    78	        public Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] T>(string? title, DialogOptions options) where T : IComponent
    79	        {
    80	            return ShowAsync<T>(title, DialogParameters.Default, options);
    81	        }
    82	
    83	        /// <inheritdoc />
    84	        public Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] T>(string? title, DialogParameters parameters) where T : IComponent
    85	        {
    86	            return ShowAsync<T>(title, parameters, DialogOptions.Default);
    87	        }
    88	
    89	        /// <inheritdoc />
    90	        public Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] T>(string? title, DialogParameters parameters,
    91	            DialogOptions? options) where T : IComponent
    92	        {
    93	            return ShowAsync(typeof(T), title, parameters, options ?? DialogOptions.Default);
    94	        }
    95	
    96	        /// <inheritdoc />
    97	        public Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync([DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] Type component)
    98	        {
    99	            return ShowAsync(component, string.Empty, DialogParameters.Default, DialogOptions.Default);
   100	        }
   101	
   102	        /// <inheritdoc />
   103	        public Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync([DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] Type component, string? title)
   104	        {
   105	            return ShowAsync(component, title, DialogParameters.Default, DialogOptions.Default);
   106	        }
   107	
   108	        /// <inheritdoc />
   109	        public Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync([DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] Type component, string? title, DialogOptions options)
   110	        {
   111	            return ShowAsync(component, title, DialogParameters.Default, options);
   112	        }
   113	
   114	        /// <inheritdoc />
   115	        public Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync([DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] Type component, string? title, DialogParameters parameters)
   116	        {
   117	            return ShowAsync(component, title, parameters, DialogOptions.Default);
   118	        }
   119	
   120	        /// <inheritdoc />
   121	        public async Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync([DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] Type component, string? title,
   122	            DialogParameters parameters, DialogOptions options)
   123	        {
   124	            var dialogReference = await ShowCoreAsync(component, title, parameters, options);
   125	
   126	            //Do not wait forever, what if render fails because of some internal exception and we will never release the method.
   127	            var cancellationTokenSource = new CancellationTokenSource(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(5));
   128	            var token = cancellationTokenSource.Token;
   129	            await using (token.Register(() => dialogReference.RenderCompleteTaskCompletionSource.TrySetResult(false)))
   130	            {
   131	                await dialogReference.RenderCompleteTaskCompletionSource.Task;
   132	
   133	                return dialogReference;
   134	            }
   135	        }
   136	
   137	        /// <inheritdoc />
   138	        public Task<bool?> ShowMessageBoxAsync(string? title, string message, string yesText = "OK",
   139	            string? noText = null, string? cancelText = null, DialogOptions? options = null)
   140	        {
   141	            return ShowMessageBoxAsync(new MessageBoxOptions
   142	            {
   143	                Title = title,
   144	                Message = message,
   145	                YesText = yesText,
   146	                NoText = noText,
   147	                CancelText = cancelText,
   148	            }, options);
   149	        }
   150	
   151	        /// <inheritdoc />
   152	        public Task<bool?> ShowMessageBoxAsync(string? title, MarkupString markupMessage, string yesText = "OK",
   153	            string? noText = null, string? cancelText = null, DialogOptions? options = null)
   154	        {
   155	            return ShowMessageBoxAsync(new MessageBoxOptions
   156	            {
   157	                Title = title,
   158	                MarkupMessage = markupMessage,
   159	                YesText = yesText,
   160	                NoText = noText,
   161	                CancelText = cancelText,
   162	            }, options);
   163	        }
   164	
   165	        /// <inheritdoc />
   166	        public async Task<bool?> ShowMessageBoxAsync(MessageBoxOptions messageBoxOptions, DialogOptions? options = null)
   167	        {
   168	            var parameters = new DialogParameters
   169	            {
   170	                [nameof(MessageBoxOptions.Title)] = messageBoxOptions.Title,
   171	                [nameof(MessageBoxOptions.Message)] = messageBoxOptions.Message,
   172	                [nameof(MessageBoxOptions.MarkupMessage)] = messageBoxOptions.MarkupMessage,
   173	                [nameof(MessageBoxOptions.CancelText)] = messageBoxOptions.CancelText,
   174	                [nameof(MessageBoxOptions.NoText)] = messageBoxOptions.NoText,
   175	                [nameof(MessageBoxOptions.YesText)] = messageBoxOptions.YesText,
   176	            };
   177	            var reference = await ShowAsync<MudMessageBox>(title: messageBoxOptions.Title, parameters: parameters, options: options);
   178	            var result = await reference.Result;
   179	
   180	            if (result is null)
   181	            {
   182	                return null;
   183	            }
   184	
   185	            if (result.Canceled || result.Data is not bool data)
   186	            {
   187	                return null;
   188	            }
   189	
   190	            return data;
   191	        }
   192	
   193	        /// <inheritdoc />
   194	        public void Close(IDialogReference dialog)
   195	        {
   196	            Close(dialog, DialogResult.Ok<object?>(null));
   197	        }
   198	
   199	        /// <inheritdoc />
   200	        public virtual void Close(IDialogReference dialog, DialogResult? result)
   201	        {
   202	            OnDialogCloseRequested?.Invoke(dialog, result);
   203	        }
   204	
   205	        /// <inheritdoc />
   206	        public virtual IDialogReference CreateReference()
   207	        {
   208	            return new DialogReference(Guid.NewGuid(), this);
   209	        }
   210	
   211	        private async Task<IDialogReference> ShowCoreAsync([DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] Type contentComponent, string? title, DialogParameters parameters,
   212	            DialogOptions options)
   213	        {
   214	            if (!typeof(IComponent).IsAssignableFrom(contentComponent))
   215	            {
   216	                throw new ArgumentException($"{contentComponent.FullName} must be a Blazor IComponent");
   217	            }
   218	
   219	            var dialogReference = CreateReference();
   220	            dialogReference.InjectOptions(options);
   221	            var dialogContent = DialogHelperComponent.Wrap(builder =>
   222	            {
   223	                var i = 0;
   224	                builder.OpenComponent(i++, contentComponent);
   225	                foreach (var parameter in parameters)
   226	                {
   227	                    builder.AddAttribute(i++, parameter.Key, parameter.Value);
   228	                }
   229	
   230	                builder.AddComponentReferenceCapture(i, inst => { dialogReference.InjectDialog(inst); });
   231	                builder.CloseComponent();
   232	            });
   233	            var dialogInstance = new RenderFragment(builder =>
   234	            {
   235	                builder.OpenComponent<MudDialogContainer>(0);
   236	                builder.SetKey(dialogReference.Id);
   237	                builder.AddComponentParameter(1, nameof(MudDialogContainer.Options), options);
   238	                builder.AddComponentParameter(2, nameof(MudDialogContainer.Title), title);
   239	                builder.AddComponentParameter(3, nameof(MudDialogContainer.Content), dialogContent);
   240	                builder.AddComponentParameter(4, nameof(MudDialogContainer.Id), dialogReference.Id);
   241	                builder.CloseComponent();
   242	            });
   243	            dialogReference.InjectRenderFragment(dialogInstance);
   244	
   245	            var dialogInstanceAddedAsync = DialogInstanceAddedAsync;
   246	            if (dialogInstanceAddedAsync is not null)
   247	            {
   248	                await dialogInstanceAddedAsync(dialogReference);
   249	            }
   250	
   251	            return dialogReference;
   252	        }
   253	    }
   254	}

     1	﻿// Copyright (c) 2019 - Blazored
     2	// Copyright (c) 2020 - Adaptations by Jonny Larsson and Meinrad Recheis
     3	
     4	using System.Collections;
     5	using System.Collections.Generic;
     6	
     7	namespace MudBlazor
     8	{
     9	    /// <summary>
    10	    /// Parameter bag for passing values into a <see cref="MudDialog"/> instance.
    11	    /// </summary>
    12	    /// <remarks>
    13	    /// Use this class to construct a strongly-named parameter set for dialog content components,
    14	    /// keeping dialog invocation code clean and explicit.
    15	    /// </remarks>
    16	    public class DialogParameters : IEnumerable<KeyValuePair<string, object?>>
    17	    {
    18	        /// <summary>
    19	        /// The default dialog parameters.
    20	        /// This field is only intended for parameters that do not differ from their default values.
    21	        /// </summary>
    22	        internal static readonly DialogParameters Default = new();
    23	
    24	        internal Dictionary<string, object?> _parameters = new();
    25	
    26	        /// <summary>
    27	        /// Adds or updates a parameter.
    28	        /// </summary>
    29	        /// <param name="parameterName">The name of the parameter.</param>
    30	        /// <param name="value">The value to add or update.</param>
    31	        public void Add(string parameterName, object? value)
    32	        {
    33	            _parameters[parameterName] = value;
    34	        }
    35	
    36	        /// <summary>
    37	        /// Gets an existing parameter.
    38	        /// </summary>
    39	        /// <typeparam name="T">The type of value to return.</typeparam>
    40	        /// <param name="parameterName">The name of the parameter to find.</param>
    41	        /// <returns>The parameter value, if it exists.</returns>
    42	        public T? Get<T>(string parameterName)
    43	        {
    44	            if (_parameters.TryGetValue(parameterName, out var value))
    45	            {
    46	                return (T?)value;
    47	            }
    48	
    49	            throw new KeyNotFoundException($"{parameterName} does not exist in Dialog parameters");
    50	        }
    51	
    52	        /// <summary>
    53	        /// Gets an existing parameter or a default value if nothing was found.
    54	        /// </summary>
    55	        /// <typeparam name="T">The type of value to return.</typeparam>
    56	        /// <param name="parameterName">The name of the parameter to find.</param>
    57	        /// <returns>The parameter value, if it exists.</returns>
    58	        public T? TryGet<T>(string parameterName)
    59	        {
    60	            if (_parameters.TryGetValue(parameterName, out var value))
    61	            {
    62	                return (T?)value;
    63	            }
    64	
    65	            return default;
    66	        }
    67	
    68	        /// <summary>
    69	        /// The number of parameters.
    70	        /// </summary>
    71	        public int Count => _parameters.Count;
    72	
    73	        /// <summary>
    74	        /// Gets or sets a parameter.
    75	        /// </summary>
    76	        /// <param name="parameterName">The name of the parameter to find.</param>
    77	        /// <returns>The parameter value.</returns>
    78	        public object? this[string parameterName]
    79	        {
    80	            get => Get<object?>(parameterName);
    81	            set => _parameters[parameterName] = value;
    82	        }
    83	
    84	        /// <summary>
    85	        /// Gets an enumerator for all parameters.
    86	        /// </summary>
    87	        /// <returns>An enumerator of <see cref="KeyValuePair{TKey, TValue}"/> values.</returns>
    88	        public IEnumerator<KeyValuePair<string, object?>> GetEnumerator()
    89	        {
    90	            return _parameters.GetEnumerator();
    91	        }
    92	
    93	        IEnumerator IEnumerable.GetEnumerator()
    94	        {
    95	            return _parameters.GetEnumerator();
    96	        }
    97	    }
    98	}

     1	﻿// Copyright (c) 2019 - Blazored
     2	// Copyright (c) 2023 - Adaptations by Simon Schulze
     3	// Copyright (c) 2024 - Adaptations by MudBlazor
     4	
     5	using System.Linq.Expressions;
     6	
     7	namespace MudBlazor;
     8	
     9	/// <summary>
    10	/// Strongly-typed dialog parameter bag for components of type <typeparamref name="T"/>.
    11	/// </summary>
    12	/// <remarks>
    13	/// Use this when you want compile-time safety for parameter names while building dialog parameters.
    14	/// </remarks>
    15	/// <seealso cref="MudDialogContainer"/>
    16	/// <seealso cref="MudDialogProvider"/>
    17	/// <seealso cref="MudDialog"/>
    18	/// <seealso cref="DialogOptions"/>
    19	/// <seealso cref="DialogReference"/>
    20	/// <seealso cref="DialogService"/>
    21	public class DialogParameters<T> : DialogParameters
    22	{
    23	    /// <summary>
    24	    /// Adds a parameter using a member expression.
    25	    /// </summary>
    26	    /// <typeparam name="TParam">The type of parameter to add.</typeparam>
    27	    /// <param name="propertyExpression">The property to add as a parameter.</param>
    28	    /// <param name="value">The parameter value.</param>
    29	    public void Add<TParam>(Expression<Func<T, TParam>> propertyExpression, TParam value)
    30	    {
    31	        ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(propertyExpression);
    32	        if (propertyExpression.Body is not MemberExpression memberExpression)
    33	        {
    34	            throw new ArgumentException($"Argument '{nameof(propertyExpression)}' must be a '{nameof(MemberExpression)}'");
    35	        }
    36	
    37	        Add(memberExpression.Member.Name, value);
    38	    }
    39	
    40	    /// <summary>
    41	    /// Gets a parameter using a property expression.
    42	    /// </summary>
    43	    /// <typeparam name="TParam">The type of parameter to get.</typeparam>
    44	    /// <param name="propertyExpression">The property to get as a parameter.</param>
    45	    /// <returns>The parameter value.</returns>
    46	    public TParam? Get<TParam>(Expression<Func<T, TParam>> propertyExpression)
    47	    {
    48	        ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(propertyExpression);
    49	        if (propertyExpression.Body is not MemberExpression memberExpression)
    50	        {
    51	            throw new ArgumentException($"Argument '{nameof(propertyExpression)}' must be a '{nameof(MemberExpression)}'");
    52	        }
    53	
    54	        return Get<TParam?>(memberExpression.Member.Name);
    55	    }
    56	
    57	    /// <summary>
    58	    /// Gets a parameter using a property expression or a default value if no parameter was found.
    59	    /// </summary>
    60	    /// <typeparam name="TParam">The type of parameter to get.</typeparam>
    61	    /// <param name="propertyExpression">The property to get as a parameter.</param>
    62	    /// <returns>The parameter value.</returns>
    63	    public TParam? TryGet<TParam>(Expression<Func<T, TParam>> propertyExpression)
    64	    {
    65	        ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(propertyExpression);
    66	        if (propertyExpression.Body is not MemberExpression memberExpression)
    67	        {
    68	            throw new ArgumentException($"Argument '{nameof(propertyExpression)}' must be a '{nameof(MemberExpression)}'");
    69	        }
    70	
    71	        return TryGet<TParam>(memberExpression.Member.Name);
    72	    }
    73	}

2026-05-29 12:04:08,288 - INFO - swea-agent-MudBlazor__MudBlazor-12908 - 💭 THOUGHT
Now I understand the structure. The PR asks to add a new overload to `IDialogService` and `DialogService`:

1. `ShowAsync<T>(DialogParameters<T> parameters)` - generic type, no title, just parameters
2. `ShowAsync<T>(DialogParameters<T> parameters, DialogOptions options)` - generic type, no title, parameters and options

Currently there are already `ShowAsync<[T]>(string? title, DialogParameters parameters)` and `ShowAsync<[T]>(string? title, DialogParameters parameters, DialogOptions? options)` but these take the non-generic `DialogParameters`. The PR specifically wants to add overloads that take the generic `DialogParameters<T>` without requiring a title.

But looking more carefully at the PR description, it says:

> `await dialogService.ShowAsync<MyDialog>(new DialogParameters<MyDialog>() { { x => x.MyParam, "value" } });`

So they want both:
1. `ShowAsync<T>(DialogParameters<T> parameters)` 
2. Maybe also `ShowAsync<T>(DialogParameters<T> parameters, DialogOptions options)`

Let me also check the test files to see what tests exist:

    19	        /// <summary>
    20	        /// Testing lifecycle of dialogs in DialogProvider
    21	        /// </summary>
    22	        [Test]
    23	        public async Task Lifecycle()
    24	        {
    25	            var comp = Context.Render<MudDialogProvider>();
    26	            var service = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<IDialogService>();
    27	            service.Should().NotBe(null);
    28	            IDialogReference dialogReference = null;
    29	            await comp.InvokeAsync(async () =>
    30	            {
    31	                dialogReference = await service.ShowAsync<DialogRender>();
    32	                var result1 = await dialogReference.Result;
    33	                //The second Dialog is added here, but the first dialog is still in the _dialogs collection of the DialogProvider, as only the result task was set to completion.
    34	                //So DialogProvider will render again with 2 dialogs, but 1 is completed. This one needs to be excluded from rendering to prevent double initialize with no params.
    35	                dialogReference = await service.ShowAsync<DialogRender>();
    36	                var result2 = await dialogReference.Result;
    37	            });
    38	            DialogRender.OnInitializedCount.Should().Be(2);
    39	            //Reset global value
    40	            DialogRender.OnInitializedCount = 0;
    41	        }

    43	        /// <summary>
    44	        /// Opening and closing a simple dialog
    45	        /// </summary>
    46	        [Test]
    47	        public async Task Simple()
    48	        {
    49	            var comp = Context.Render<MudDialogProvider>();
    50	            comp.Markup.Trim().Should().BeEmpty();
    51	            var service = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<IDialogService>();
    52	            service.Should().NotBe(null);
    53	            IDialogReference dialogReference = null;
    54	            // open simple test dialog
    55	            await comp.InvokeAsync(async () => dialogReference = await service.ShowAsync<DialogOkCancel>());
    56	            dialogReference.Should().NotBe(null);
    57	            comp.Find("div.mud-dialog-container").Should().NotBe(null);
    58	            comp.Find("p.mud-typography").TrimmedText().Should().Be("Wabalabadubdub!");
    59	            // close by click outside the dialog
    60	            comp.Find("div.mud-overlay").Click();
    61	            comp.Markup.Trim().Should().BeEmpty();
    62	            var result = await dialogReference.Result;
    63	            result.Canceled.Should().BeTrue();
    64	            // open simple test dialog
    65	            await comp.InvokeAsync(async () => dialogReference = await service.ShowAsync<DialogOkCancel>());
    66	            // close by click on cancel button
    67	            comp.FindAll("button")[0].Click();
    68	            result = await dialogReference.Result;
    69	            result.Canceled.Should().BeTrue();
    70	            // open simple test dialog
    71	            await comp.InvokeAsync(async () => dialogReference = await service.ShowAsync<DialogOkCancel>());
    72	            // close by click on ok button
    73	            comp.FindAll("button")[1].Click();
    74	            result = await dialogReference.Result;
    75	            result.Canceled.Should().BeFalse();
    76	
    77	            //create 2 instances and dismiss all
    78	            await comp.InvokeAsync(async () => dialogReference = await service.ShowAsync<DialogOkCancel>());
    79	            await comp.InvokeAsync(async () => dialogReference = await service.ShowAsync<DialogOkCancel>());
    80	            var cont = comp.FindAll("div.mud-dialog-container");
    81	            cont.Count.Should().Be(2);
    82	            await comp.InvokeAsync(() => comp.Instance.DismissAll());
    83	            cont = comp.FindAll("div.mud-dialog-container");
    84	            cont.Count.Should().Be(0);
    85	
    86	            // Close by using default close method
    87	            await comp.InvokeAsync(async () => dialogReference = await service.ShowAsync<DialogOkCancel>());
    88	            comp.FindAll("button")[2].Click();
    89	            result = await dialogReference.Result;
    90	            result.Data.Should().BeNull();
    91	            result.DataType.Should().BeNull();
    92	            result.Canceled.Should().BeFalse();
    93	        }

    95	        /// <summary>
    96	        /// Opening and closing dialogs via navigation.
    97	        /// </summary>
    98	        [Test]
    99	        public async Task CloseOnNavigationTest()
   100	        {
   101	            var comp = Context.Render<MudDialogProvider>();
   102	            comp.Markup.Trim().Should().BeEmpty();
   103	            var dialogService = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<IDialogService>();
   104	            dialogService.Should().NotBe(null);
   105	            var navigationManager = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<NavigationManager>();
   106	            navigationManager.Should().NotBe(null);
   107	
   108	            //create 2 instances and dismiss all except for one with CloseOnNavigation = false
   109	            var closeOnNavigationOptions = new DialogOptions
   110	            {
   111	                CloseOnNavigation = false
   112	            };
   113	            await comp.InvokeAsync(async () => _ = await dialogService.ShowAsync<DialogOkCancel>("test", options: closeOnNavigationOptions));
   114	            await comp.InvokeAsync(async () => _ = await dialogService.ShowAsync<DialogOkCancel>());
   115	            var cont = comp.FindAll("div.mud-dialog-container");
   116	            cont.Count.Should().Be(2);
   117	
   118	            var uri = new Uri(navigationManager.Uri);
   119	            var query = HttpUtility.ParseQueryString(uri.Query);
   120	            query["query"] = Guid.NewGuid().ToString();
   121	            var newUri = $"{uri.GetLeftPart(UriPartial.Path)}?{query}";
   122	            await comp.InvokeAsync(() => navigationManager.NavigateTo(newUri));
   123	
   124	            cont = comp.FindAll("div.mud-dialog-container");
   125	            cont.Count.Should().Be(1);
   126	        }

   128	        /// <summary>
   129	        /// <para>Opening and closing an inline dialog. Click on open will open the inlined dialog.</para>
   130	        /// <para>
   131	        /// Note: this test uses two different components, one containing the dialog provider and
   132	        /// one containing the open button and the inline dialog
   133	        /// </para>
   134	        /// </summary>
   135	        [Test]
   136	        public async Task InlineDialog()
   137	        {
   138	            var comp = Context.Render<MudDialogProvider>();
   139	            comp.Markup.Trim().Should().BeEmpty();
   140	            var service = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<IDialogService>();
   141	            service.Should().NotBe(null);
   142	            // displaying the component with the inline dialog only renders the open button
   143	            var comp1 = Context.Render<TestInlineDialog>();
   144	            comp1.FindComponents<MudButton>().Count.Should().Be(1);
   145	            // open the dialog
   146	            comp1.Find("button").Click();
   147	            await comp1.WaitForAssertionAsync(() =>
   148	                comp.Find("div.mud-dialog-container").Should().NotBe(null)
   149	            );
   150	            comp.Find("p.mud-typography").TrimmedText().Should().Be("Wabalabadubdub!");
   151	            comp.Find("div.mud-dialog").GetAttribute("class").Should().Contain("mud-dialog-width-full");
   152	            // close by click on ok button
   153	            comp.Find("button").Click();
   154	            comp.Markup.Trim().Should().BeEmpty();
   155	        }

   157	        /// <summary>
   158	        /// https://github.com/MudBlazor/MudBlazor/issues/4098
   159	        /// https://github.com/MudBlazor/MudBlazor/issues/8746
   160	        /// </summary>
   161	        [Test]
   162	        public async Task InlineDialogShowMethod()
   163	        {
   164	            var comp = Context.Render<MudDialogProvider>();
   165	            comp.Markup.Trim().Should().BeEmpty();
   166	            var service = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<IDialogService>();
   167	            service.Should().NotBe(null);
   168	
   169	            var comp1 = Context.Render<InlineDialogShowMethod>();
   170	
   171	            comp.Markup.Should().NotContain("Here be dragons");
   172	
   173	            // open the dialog
   174	            comp1.Find(".open-dialog-button").Click();
   175	            await comp1.WaitForAssertionAsync(() => comp.Find("div.mud-dialog-container").Should().NotBe(null));
   176	
   177	            comp.Markup.Should().Contain("Here be dragons");
   178	
   179	            // close the dialog
   180	            comp.Find(".close-dialog-button").Click();
   181	
   182	            // messagebox should have opened
   183	            comp.Markup.Should().Contain("dialog was successfully closed");
   184	
   185	            // close by click on ok button
   186	            comp.Find(".mud-message-box button").Click();
   187	
   188	            await comp.WaitForAssertionAsync(() => comp.Markup.Trim().Should().BeEmpty(), timeout: TimeSpan.FromSeconds(5));
   189	        }

   191	        /// <summary>
   192	        /// Check where a dialog is opened, canceled and reopened.
   193	        /// </summary>
   194	        /// <remarks>
   195	        /// https://github.com/MudBlazor/MudBlazor/issues/11789
   196	        /// </remarks>
   197	        [Test]
   198	        public void InlineDialog_OpenCancelOpen()
   199	        {
   200	            // Arrange
   201	
   202	            var comp = Context.Render<MudDialogProvider>();
   203	            var sup = Context.Render<InlineDialogShowMethodTest>();
   204	
   205	            // Assert : Initial state, dialog should be closed
   206	
   207	            comp.FindAll(".mud-dialog-content").Should().BeEmpty();
   208	
   209	            // Act : Open the dialog
   210	
   211	            sup.Find(".open-dialog-button").Click();
   212	
   213	            // Assert : Dialog should be open
   214	
   215	            comp.Find(".mud-dialog-content").InnerHtml.Trim().Should().NotBeEmpty();
   216	
   217	            // Act : Cancel by click outside
   218	
   219	            comp.Find("div.mud-overlay-dialog").Click();
   220	
   221	            // Assert : Dialog should be closed
   222	
   223	            comp.FindAll(".mud-dialog-content").Should().BeEmpty();
   224	
   225	            // Act : Reopen the dialog
   226	
   227	            sup.Find(".open-dialog-button").Click();
   228	
   229	            // Assert : Dialog should be open
   230	
   231	            comp.Find(".mud-dialog-content").InnerHtml.Trim().Should().NotBeEmpty();
   232	        }

   234	        /// <summary>
   235	        /// Nested dialogs should not appear unless manually shown
   236	        /// </summary>
   237	        [Test]
   238	        public async Task NestedInlineDialog()
   239	        {
   240	            var provider = Context.Render<MudDialogProvider>();
   241	            provider.Markup.Trim().Should().BeEmpty();
   242	            var service = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<IDialogService>();
   243	            service.Should().NotBe(null);
   244	            // displaying the component with the inline dialog only renders the open button
   245	            var comp = Context.Render<TestNestedInlineDialog>();
   246	            comp.FindComponents<MudButton>().Count.Should().Be(1);
   247	            // open the dialog
   248	            comp.Find("button").Click();
   249	            await comp.WaitForAssertionAsync(() =>
   250	                provider.Find("div.mud-dialog-container").Should().NotBe(null)
   251	            );
   252	            provider.Find("p.mud-typography").TrimmedText().Should().Be("Scorpiany!");
   253	            provider.FindComponents<MudText>().Count.Should().Be(2); //counts both the dialog header and the text in our test component
   254	
   255	            provider.Find("button").Click(); //open nested dialog
   256	            await comp.WaitForAssertionAsync(() =>
   257	                provider.Find(".nested").Should().NotBe(null)
   258	            );
   259	
   260	            provider.FindAll("p.mud-typography")[1].TrimmedText().Should().Be("Nested dialog!");
   261	            provider.FindComponents<MudText>().Count.Should().Be(4); //now we have another MudText
   262	        }

   264	        /// <summary>
   265	        /// Click outside the dialog (or any other method) must update the Visible parameter two-way binding on close
   266	        /// </summary>
   267	        /// <returns></returns>
   268	        [Test]
   269	        public async Task InlineDialog_Should_UpdateIsVisibleOnClose()
   270	        {
   271	            var comp = Context.Render<MudDialogProvider>();
   272	            comp.Markup.Trim().Should().BeEmpty();
   273	            var service = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<IDialogService>();
   274	            service.Should().NotBe(null);
   275	            var comp1 = Context.Render<InlineDialogIsVisibleStateTest>();
   276	            await comp1.InvokeAsync(() => comp1.Find("button").Click());
   277	            await comp.WaitForAssertionAsync(() => comp.FindAll("div.mud-dialog-container").Count.Should().Be(1));
   278	            await comp.InvokeAsync(() => comp.WaitForElement("div.mud-overlay").Click());
   279	            await comp.WaitForAssertionAsync(() => comp.Markup.Trim().Should().BeEmpty(), TimeSpan.FromSeconds(5));
   280	            await comp1.InvokeAsync(() => comp1.Find("button").Click());
   281	            await comp.WaitForAssertionAsync(() => comp.FindAll("div.mud-dialog-container").Count.Should().Be(1),
   282	                TimeSpan.FromSeconds(5));
   283	            await comp.InvokeAsync(() => comp.WaitForElement("div.mud-overlay").Click());
   284	            await comp.WaitForAssertionAsync(() => comp.Markup.Trim().Should().BeEmpty(), TimeSpan.FromSeconds(5));
   285	        }

   308	        /// <summary>
   309	        /// Based on bug report by Porkopek:
   310	        /// Updating values that are referenced in TitleContent render fragment won't result in an update of the dialog title
   311	        /// when they change. This is solved by allowing the user to call ForceRender() on DialogInstance
   312	        /// </summary>
   313	        /// <returns></returns>
   314	        [Test]
   315	        public async Task DialogShouldUpdateTitleContent()
   316	        {
   317	            var comp = Context.Render<MudDialogProvider>();
   318	            comp.Markup.Trim().Should().BeEmpty();
   319	            var service = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<IDialogService>();
   320	            service.Should().NotBe(null);
   321	            IDialogReference dialogReference = null;
   322	            // open simple test dialog
   323	            await comp.InvokeAsync(async () => dialogReference = await service.ShowAsync<DialogThatUpdatesItsTitle>());
   324	            dialogReference.Should().NotBe(null);
   325	            //comp.Find("div.mud-dialog-container").Should().NotBe(null);
   326	            comp.Find("div.mud-dialog-content").TrimmedText().Should().Be("Body:");
   327	            comp.Find("div.mud-dialog-title").TrimmedText().Should().Be("Title:");
   328	            // click on ok button should set title and content
   329	            comp.FindAll("button")[1].Click();
   330	            comp.Find("div.mud-dialog-content").TrimmedText().Should().Be("Body: Test123");
   331	            comp.Find("div.mud-dialog-title").TrimmedText().Should().Be("Title: Test123");
   332	        }

   334	        /// <summary>
   335	        /// A test that ensures parameters are not overwritten when dialog is updated
   336	        /// </summary>
   337	        [Test]
   338	        public async Task DialogShouldNotOverwriteParameters()
   339	        {
   340	            var comp = Context.Render<MudDialogProvider>();
   341	            comp.Markup.Trim().Should().BeEmpty();
   342	            var service = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<IDialogService>();
   343	            service.Should().NotBe(null);
   344	            IDialogReference dialogReference = null;
   345	
   346	            var parameters = new DialogParameters<DialogWithParameters>
   347	            {
   348	                { x => x.TestValue, "test" },
   349	                { x => x.ColorTest, Color.Error } // !! comment me !!
   350	            };
   351	
   352	            await comp.InvokeAsync(async () => dialogReference = await service.ShowAsync<DialogWithParameters>(string.Empty, parameters));
   353	            dialogReference.Should().NotBe(null);
   354	
   355	            var textField = comp.FindComponent<MudInput<string>>().Instance;
   356	            textField.ReadText.Should().Be("test");
   357	
   358	            comp.Find("input").Change("new_test");
   359	            comp.Find("input").Blur();
   360	            textField.ReadText.Should().Be("new_test");
   361	
   362	            comp.FindAll("button")[0].Click();
   363	
   364	            ((DialogWithParameters)dialogReference.Dialog).TestValue.Should().Be("new_test");
   365	            ((DialogWithParameters)dialogReference.Dialog).ParametersSetCounter.Should().Be(1);
   366	            textField.ReadText.Should().Be("new_test");
   367	        }

   369	        [Test]
   370	        public async Task PassingEventCallbackToDialogViaParameters()
   371	        {
   372	            var comp = Context.Render<MudDialogProvider>();
   373	            comp.Markup.Trim().Should().BeEmpty();
   374	            var service = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<IDialogService>();
   375	            service.Should().NotBe(null);
   376	
   377	            var testComp = Context.Render<DialogWithEventCallbackTest>();
   378	            // open dialog
   379	            testComp.Find("button").Click();
   380	            // in the opened dialog find the text field
   381	            var tf = comp.FindComponent<MudTextField<string>>();
   382	            tf.Find("input").Input("User input ...");
   383	            // the user input should be passed out of the dialog into the outer component and displayed there.
   384	            await testComp.WaitForAssertionAsync(() =>
   385	                testComp.Find("p").TextContent.Trim().Should().Be("Search Text:  User input ...")
   386	            );
   387	        }
   388	
   389	        [Test]
   390	        public async Task CustomDialogService()
   391	        {
   392	            //Remove default IDialogService so we can provide our custom implementation
   393	            //This is not necessary in normal cases, you would rather just not register all services in the beginning, but the test environment requires here to do so.
   394	            Context.Services.Remove(Context.Services.FirstOrDefault(descriptor => descriptor.ServiceType == typeof(IDialogService)));
   395	            //Register our custom dialog service implementation as the new service instance behind IDialogService
   396	            Context.Services.AddScoped<IDialogService>(sp => new CustomDialogService());
   397	
   398	            //Render our dialog provider and make sure everything is fine
   399	            var comp = Context.Render<MudDialogProvider>();
   400	            comp.Markup.Trim().Should().BeEmpty();
   401	
   402	            //Try to get the current service instance for the type IDialogService and make sure it is our custom implementation
   403	            var service = Context.Services.GetService<IDialogService>();
   404	            service.Should().NotBe(null);
   405	            service.Should().BeAssignableTo(typeof(CustomDialogService));
   406	
   407	            //Show the dialog, create reference and make sure it is our custom dialog reference implementation
   408	            IDialogReference dialogReference = null;
   409	            //The type of the dialog does not really matter, for the sake of laziness I will re-use this existing one
   410	            await comp.InvokeAsync(async () => dialogReference = await service?.ShowAsync<DialogThatUpdatesItsTitle>());
   411	            dialogReference.Should().NotBe(null);
   412	            dialogReference.Should().BeAssignableTo(typeof(CustomDialogReference));
   413	
   414	            //After above checks have passed, we can safely cast the generic reference into our specific implementation
   415	            var customDialogReference = (CustomDialogReference)dialogReference;
   416	            //The custom property should be false by default otherwise the rest of the test logic would be incorrect
   417	            customDialogReference.AllowDismiss.Should().BeFalse();
   418	
   419	            //Dialog should not be closable through backdrop click
   420	            comp.Find("div.mud-overlay").Click();
   421	            await comp.WaitForAssertionAsync(() => comp.Markup.Trim().Should().NotBeEmpty(), TimeSpan.FromSeconds(5));
   422	
   423	            //Allow dismiss
   424	            customDialogReference.AllowDismiss = true;
   425	
   426	            //Dialog should now be closable through backdrop click
   427	            comp.Find("div.mud-overlay").Click();
   428	            await comp.WaitForAssertionAsync(() => comp.Markup.Trim().Should().BeEmpty(), TimeSpan.FromSeconds(5));
   429	        }

   431	        /// <summary>
   432	        /// Getting return value from dialog
   433	        /// </summary>
   434	        [Test]
   435	        public async Task DialogShouldReturnTheReturnValue()
   436	        {
   437	            var comp = Context.Render<MudDialogProvider>();
   438	            comp.Markup.Trim().Should().BeEmpty();
   439	            var service = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<IDialogService>();
   440	            service.Should().NotBe(null);
   441	            IDialogReference dialogReference = null;
   442	            // open dialog
   443	            await comp.InvokeAsync(async () => dialogReference = await service.ShowAsync<DialogWithReturnValue>());
   444	            dialogReference.Should().NotBe(null);
   445	            // close by click on cancel button
   446	            comp.FindAll("button")[0].Click();
   447	            var rv = await dialogReference.GetReturnValueAsync<string>();
   448	            rv.Should().BeNull();
   449	            // open dialog
   450	            await comp.InvokeAsync(async () => dialogReference = await service.ShowAsync<DialogWithReturnValue>());
   451	            // close by click on ok button
   452	            comp.FindAll("button")[1].Click();
   453	            rv = await dialogReference.GetReturnValueAsync<string>();
   454	            rv.Should().Be("Closed via OK");
   455	        }

   457	        [Test]
   458	        public async Task DialogKeyboardNavigation()
   459	        {
   460	            var keyInterceptorService = Context.AddKeyInterceptorService();
   461	            var comp = Context.Render<MudDialogProvider>();
   462	            comp.Markup.Trim().Should().BeEmpty();
   463	            var service = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<IDialogService>();
   464	            service.Should().NotBe(null);
   465	            IDialogReference dialogReference = null;
   466	            //dialog with clickable backdrop
   467	            await comp.InvokeAsync(async () => dialogReference = await service.ShowAsync<DialogOkCancel>(string.Empty, new DialogOptions() { CloseOnEscapeKey = true }));
   468	            dialogReference.Should().NotBe(null);
   469	            var dialog1 = (DialogOkCancel)dialogReference.Dialog!;
   470	            var dialogInstance1 = dialog1.MudDialog.GetDialogContainer();
   471	            comp.Markup.Trim().Should().NotBeEmpty();
   472	
   473	            await comp.InvokeAsync(() => keyInterceptorService.OnKeyDown(dialogInstance1.ElementId, new KeyboardEventArgs { Key = "Escape", Type = "keydown", }));
   474	            comp.Markup.Trim().Should().BeEmpty();
   475	            //dialog with disabled backdrop click
   476	            await comp.InvokeAsync(async () => dialogReference = await service.ShowAsync<DialogOkCancel>(string.Empty, new DialogOptions() { CloseOnEscapeKey = false }));
   477	            dialogReference.Should().NotBe(null);
   478	            var dialog2 = (DialogOkCancel)dialogReference.Dialog!;
   479	            var dialogInstance2 = dialog2.MudDialog.GetDialogContainer();
   480	            comp.Markup.Trim().Should().NotBeEmpty();
   481	            await comp.InvokeAsync(() => keyInterceptorService.OnKeyDown(dialogInstance2.ElementId, new KeyboardEventArgs { Key = "Escape", Type = "keydown", }));
   482	            comp.Markup.Trim().Should().NotBeEmpty();
   483	        }
   484	
   485	        [Test]
   486	        public async Task DialogKeyboardEvents()
   487	        {
   488	            var keyInterceptorService = Context.AddKeyInterceptorService();
   489	            var comp = Context.Render<MudDialogProvider>();
   490	            comp.Markup.Trim().Should().BeEmpty();
   491	            var service = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<IDialogService>();
   492	            service.Should().NotBe(null);
   493	            IDialogReference dialogReference = null;
   494	            //dialog with clickable backdrop
   495	            await comp.InvokeAsync(async () => dialogReference = await service.ShowAsync<DialogOkCancel>(string.Empty, new DialogOptions() { CloseOnEscapeKey = true }));
   496	            dialogReference.Should().NotBe(null);
   497	            var dialog1 = ((DialogOkCancel)dialogReference.Dialog)!;
   498	            var dialogInstance = dialog1.MudDialog.GetDialogContainer();
   499	            dialog1.LastKeyDown.Should().Be(null);
   500	            dialog1.LastKeyUp.Should().Be(null);
   501	            comp.Markup.Trim().Should().NotBeEmpty();
   502	
   503	            await comp.InvokeAsync(async () => await keyInterceptorService.OnKeyDown(dialogInstance.ElementId, new KeyboardEventArgs { Key = "Enter", Type = "keydown", }));
   504	            dialog1.LastKeyDown.Key.Should().Be("Enter");
   505	            await comp.InvokeAsync(async () => await keyInterceptorService.OnKeyUp(dialogInstance.ElementId, new KeyboardEventArgs { Key = "Backspace", Type = "keyup", }));
   506	            dialog1.LastKeyUp.Key.Should().Be("Backspace");
   507	            comp.Markup.Trim().Should().NotBeEmpty();
   508	        }

   530	        /// <summary>
   531	        /// Open Inline Dialog and the from the inline dialog another normal dialog
   532	        /// while closing the inline dialog.
   533	        /// https://github.com/MudBlazor/MudBlazor/issues/4871
   534	        /// </summary>
   535	        [Test]
   536	        public async Task InlineDialogBug4871()
   537	        {
   538	            var comp = Context.Render<MudDialogProvider>();
   539	            comp.Markup.Trim().Should().BeEmpty();
   540	            var service = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<IDialogService>();
   541	            service.Should().NotBe(null);
   542	            // displaying the component with the inline dialog only renders the open button
   543	            var comp1 = Context.Render<TestInlineDialog>();
   544	            comp1.FindComponents<MudButton>().Count.Should().Be(1);
   545	            // open the dialog
   546	            comp1.Find("button").Click();
   547	            await comp1.WaitForAssertionAsync(() =>
   548	                comp.Find("div.mud-dialog-container").Should().NotBe(null)
   549	            );
   550	            comp.Find("p.mud-typography").TrimmedText().Should().Be("Wabalabadubdub!");
   551	            comp.Find("div.mud-dialog").GetAttribute("class").Should().Contain("mud-dialog-width-full");
   552	            // close by click on ok button
   553	            comp.FindAll("button").Last().Click();
   554	            await comp.WaitForAssertionAsync(() => comp.FindComponent<MudMessageBox>());
   555	            var messageBox = comp.FindComponent<MudMessageBox>();
   556	            messageBox.Should().NotBeNull();
   557	            messageBox.Instance.YesText.Should().Be("BUG4871");
   558	        }

   561	        public async Task DialogToggleFullscreenOptions()
   562	        {
   563	            var comp = Context.Render<MudDialogProvider>();
   564	            comp.Markup.Trim().Should().BeEmpty();
   565	
   566	            var service = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<IDialogService>();
   567	            service.Should().NotBe(null);
   568	            IDialogReference dialogReference = null;
   569	
   570	            await comp.InvokeAsync(async () => dialogReference = await service.ShowAsync<DialogToggleFullscreen>());
   571	            dialogReference.Should().NotBe(null);
   572	
   573	            comp.Find("div.mud-dialog").GetAttribute("class").Should().Be("mud-dialog mud-dialog-width-sm");
   574	            comp.Find("button").Click();
   575	            comp.Find("div.mud-dialog").GetAttribute("class").Should().Be("mud-dialog mud-dialog-fullscreen");
   576	            comp.Find("button").Click();
   577	            comp.Find("div.mud-dialog").GetAttribute("class").Should().Be("mud-dialog mud-dialog-width-sm");

   580	        ///-------------------------------
   581	        /// <summary>
   582	        /// Testing lifecycle of dialogs in dialogprovider
   583	        /// </summary>
   584	        [Test]
   585	        public async Task AsyncLifecycle()
   586	        {
   587	            var comp = Context.Render<MudDialogProvider>();
   588	            var service = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<IDialogService>();
   589	            service.Should().NotBe(null);
   590	            await comp.InvokeAsync(async () =>
   591	            {
   592	                var dialogReference = await service.ShowAsync<DialogRender>();
   593	                await dialogReference.Result;
   594	                //The second Dialog is added here, but the first dialog is still in the _dialogs collection of the dialogprovider, as only the result task was set to completion.
   595	                //So DialogProvider will render again with 2 dialogs, but 1 is completed. This one needs to be excluded from rendering to prevent double initialize with no params.
   596	                dialogReference = await service.ShowAsync<DialogRender>();
   597	                await dialogReference.Result;
   598	            });
   599	            DialogRender.OnInitializedCount.Should().Be(2);
   600	            //Reset global value
   601	            DialogRender.OnInitializedCount = 0;
   602	        }

   616

   622	        /// <summary>
   623	        /// Opening and closing a simple dialog
   624	        /// </summary>
   625	        [Test]
   626	        public async Task AsyncSimple()
   627	        {
   628	            var comp = Context.Render<MudDialogProvider>();
   629	            comp.Markup.Trim().Should().BeEmpty();
   630	            var service = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<IDialogService>();
   631	            service.Should().NotBe(null);
   632	            var dialogReferenceLazy = new Lazy<Task<IDialogReference>>(() => service?.ShowAsync<DialogOkCancel>());
   633	            //open simple test dialog
   634	            await comp.InvokeAsync(() => dialogReferenceLazy.Value);
   635	            var dialogReference = await dialogReferenceLazy.Value;
   636	            dialogReference.Should().NotBe(null);
   637	            comp.Find("div.mud-dialog-container").Should().NotBe(null);
   638	            comp.Find("p.mud-typography").TrimmedText().Should().Be("Wabalabadubdub!");
   639	            //close by click outside the dialog
   640	            comp.Find("div.mud-overlay").Click();
   641	            comp.Markup.Trim().Should().BeEmpty();
   642	            var result = await dialogReference.Result;
   643	            result.Canceled.Should().BeTrue();
   644	            //open simple test dialog
   645	            dialogReferenceLazy = new Lazy<Task<IDialogReference>>(() => service.ShowAsync<DialogOkCancel>());
   646	            await comp.InvokeAsync(() => dialogReferenceLazy.Value);
   647	            dialogReference = await dialogReferenceLazy.Value;
   648	            //close by click on cancel button
   649	            comp.FindAll("button")[0].Click();
   650	            result = await dialogReference.Result;
   651	            result.Canceled.Should().BeTrue();
   652	            //open simple test dialog
   653	            dialogReferenceLazy = new Lazy<Task<IDialogReference>>(() => service.ShowAsync<DialogOkCancel>());
   654	            await comp.InvokeAsync(() => dialogReferenceLazy.Value);
   655	            dialogReference = await dialogReferenceLazy.Value;
   656	            //close by click on ok button
   657	            comp.FindAll("button")[1].Click();
   658	            result = result = await dialogReference.Result;
   659	            result.Canceled.Should().BeFalse();
   660	
   661	            //create 2 instances and dismiss all
   662	            dialogReferenceLazy = new Lazy<Task<IDialogReference>>(() => service.ShowAsync<DialogOkCancel>());
   663	            await comp.InvokeAsync(() => dialogReferenceLazy.Value);
   664	            dialogReferenceLazy = new Lazy<Task<IDialogReference>>(() => service.ShowAsync<DialogOkCancel>());
   665	            await comp.InvokeAsync(() => dialogReferenceLazy.Value);
   666	            var cont = comp.FindAll("div.mud-dialog-container");
   667	            cont.Count.Should().Be(2);
   668	            await comp.InvokeAsync(() => comp.Instance.DismissAll());
   669	            cont = comp.FindAll("div.mud-dialog-container");
   670	            cont.Count.Should().Be(0);
   671	        }

   673	        /// <summary>
   674	        /// Based on bug report #3128
   675	        /// Dialog Class parameters should be honored for inline dialog
   676	        /// </summary>
   677	        [Test]
   678	        public async Task InlineAsyncDialogShouldHonorClass()
   679	        {
   680	            var comp = Context.Render<MudDialogProvider>();
   681	            comp.Markup.Trim().Should().BeEmpty();
   682	            var service = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<IDialogService>();
   683	            service.Should().NotBe(null);
   684	            var dialogReferenceLazy = new Lazy<Task<IDialogReference>>(service.ShowAsync<TestInlineDialog>);
   685	            // open simple test dialog
   686	            await comp.InvokeAsync(() => dialogReferenceLazy.Value);
   687	            var dialogReference = await dialogReferenceLazy.Value;
   688	            await comp.WaitForAssertionAsync(() => dialogReference.Should().NotBe(null));
   689	            comp.Find("button").Click();
   690	            await comp.WaitForAssertionAsync(() => comp.Find("div.mud-dialog").ClassList.Should().Contain("test-class"));
   691	            comp.Find("div.mud-dialog-content").ClassList.Should().NotContain("test-class");
   692	            comp.Find("div.mud-dialog-content").ClassList.Should().Contain("content-class");
   693	            // check if tag is ok
   694	            var dialogInstance = comp.FindComponent<MudDialog>().Instance;
   695	            dialogInstance.Tag.Should().Be("test-tag");
   696	        }

   698	        /// <summary>
   699	        /// Based on bug report by Porkopek:
   700	        /// Updating values that are referenced in TitleContent render fragment won't result in an update of the dialog title
   701	        /// when they change. This is solved by allowing the user to call ForceRender() on DialogInstance
   702	        /// </summary>
   703	        /// <returns></returns>
   704	        [Test]
   705	        public async Task AsyncDialogShouldUpdateTitleContent()
   706	        {
   707	            var comp = Context.Render<MudDialogProvider>();
   708	            comp.Markup.Trim().Should().BeEmpty();
   709	            var service = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<IDialogService>();
   710	            service.Should().NotBe(null);
   711	            var dialogReferenceLazy = new Lazy<Task<IDialogReference>>(service.ShowAsync<DialogThatUpdatesItsTitle>);
   712	            // open simple test dialog
   713	            await comp.InvokeAsync(() => dialogReferenceLazy.Value);
   714	            var dialogReference = await dialogReferenceLazy.Value;
   715	            dialogReference.Should().NotBe(null);
   716	            //comp.Find("div.mud-dialog-container").Should().NotBe(null);
   717	            comp.Find("div.mud-dialog-content").TrimmedText().Should().Be("Body:");
   718	            comp.Find("div.mud-dialog-title").TrimmedText().Should().Be("Title:");
   719	            // click on ok button should set title and content
   720	            comp.FindAll("button")[1].Click();
   721	            comp.Find("div.mud-dialog-content").TrimmedText().Should().Be("Body: Test123");
   722	            comp.Find("div.mud-dialog-title").TrimmedText().Should().Be("Title: Test123");
   723	        }

   725	        /// <summary>
   726	        /// A test that ensures parameters are not overwritten when dialog is updated
   727	        /// </summary>
   728	        [Test]
   729	        public async Task AsyncDialogShouldNotOverwriteParameters()
   730	        {
   731	            var comp = Context.Render<MudDialogProvider>();
   732	            comp.Markup.Trim().Should().BeEmpty();
   733	            var service = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<IDialogService>();
   734	            service.Should().NotBe(null);
   735	
   736	            var parameters = new DialogParameters<DialogWithParameters>
   737	            {
   738	                { x => x.TestValue, "test" },
   739	                { x => x.ColorTest, Color.Error } // !! comment me !!
   740	            };
   741	
   742	            var dialogReferenceLazy = new Lazy<Task<IDialogReference>>(() => service.ShowAsync<DialogWithParameters>(string.Empty, parameters));
   743	            await comp.InvokeAsync(() => dialogReferenceLazy.Value);
   744	            var dialogReference = await dialogReferenceLazy.Value;
   745	            dialogReference.Should().NotBe(null);
   746	
   747	            var textField = comp.FindComponent<MudInput<string>>().Instance;
   748	            textField.ReadText.Should().Be("test");
   749	
   750	            comp.Find("input").Change("new_test");
   751	            comp.Find("input").Blur();
   752	            textField.ReadText.Should().Be("new_test");
   753	
   754	            comp.FindAll("button")[0].Click();
   755	
   756	            ((DialogWithParameters)dialogReference.Dialog).TestValue.Should().Be("new_test");
   757	            ((DialogWithParameters)dialogReference.Dialog).ParametersSetCounter.Should().Be(1);
   758	            textField.ReadText.Should().Be("new_test");
   759	        }

   761	        /// <summary>
   762	        /// Based on bug report #1385
   763	        /// Dialog Class parameters should be honored
   764	        /// </summary>
   765	        [Test]
   766	        public async Task AsyncDialogShouldHonorClass()
   767	        {
   768	            var comp = Context.Render<MudDialogProvider>();
   769	            comp.Markup.Trim().Should().BeEmpty();
   770	            var service = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<IDialogService>();
   771	            service.Should().NotBe(null);
   772	            var dialogReferenceLazy = new Lazy<Task<IDialogReference>>(service.ShowAsync<DialogOkCancel>);
   773	            // open simple test dialog
   774	            await comp.InvokeAsync(() => dialogReferenceLazy.Value);
   775	            var dialogReference = await dialogReferenceLazy.Value;
   776	            dialogReference.Should().NotBe(null);
   777	            comp.Find("div.mud-dialog").ClassList.Should().Contain("test-class");
   778	            comp.Find("div.mud-dialog-content").ClassList.Should().NotContain("test-class");
   779	            comp.Find("div.mud-dialog-content").ClassList.Should().Contain("content-class");
   780	        }
   781	
   782	        [Test]
   783	        public async Task AsyncCustomDialogService()
   784	        {
   785	            //Remove default IDialogService so we can provide our custom implementation
   786	            //This is not necessary in normal cases, you would rather just not register all services in the beginning, but the test environment requires here to do so.
   787	            Context.Services.Remove(Context.Services.FirstOrDefault(descriptor => descriptor.ServiceType == typeof(IDialogService)));
   788	            //Register our custom dialog service implementation as the new service instance behind IDialogService
   789	            Context.Services.AddScoped<IDialogService>(sp => new CustomDialogService());
   790	
   791	            //Render our dialog provider and make sure everything is fine
   792	            var comp = Context.Render<MudDialogProvider>();
   793	            comp.Markup.Trim().Should().BeEmpty();
   794	
   795	            //Try to get the current service instance for the type IDialogService and make sure it is our custom implementation
   796	            var service = Context.Services.GetService<IDialogService>();
   797	            service.Should().NotBe(null);
   798	            service.Should().BeAssignableTo(typeof(CustomDialogService));
   799	
   800	            //Show the dialog, create reference and make sure it is our custom dialog reference implementation
   801	            var dialogReferenceLazy = new Lazy<Task<IDialogReference>>(() => service?.ShowAsync<DialogThatUpdatesItsTitle>());
   802	            //The type of the dialog does not really matter, for the sake of laziness I will re-use this existing one
   803	            await comp.InvokeAsync(() => dialogReferenceLazy.Value);
   804	            var dialogReference = await dialogReferenceLazy.Value;
   805	            dialogReference.Should().NotBe(null);
   806	            dialogReference.Should().BeAssignableTo(typeof(CustomDialogReference));
   807	
   808	            //After above checks have passed, we can safely cast the generic reference into our specific implementation
   809	            var customDialogReference = (CustomDialogReference)dialogReference;
   810	            //The custom property should be false by default otherwise the rest of the test logic would be incorrect
   811	            customDialogReference.AllowDismiss.Should().BeFalse();
   812	
   813	            //Dialog should not be closable through backdrop click
   814	            comp.Find("div.mud-overlay").Click();
   815	            await comp.WaitForAssertionAsync(() => comp.Markup.Trim().Should().NotBeEmpty(), TimeSpan.FromSeconds(5));
   816	
   817	            //Allow dismiss
   818	            customDialogReference.AllowDismiss = true;
   819	
   820	            //Dialog should now be closable through backdrop click
   821	            comp.Find("div.mud-overlay").Click();
   822	            await comp.WaitForAssertionAsync(() => comp.Markup.Trim().Should().BeEmpty(), TimeSpan.FromSeconds(5));
   823	        }
   824	
   825	        /// <summary>
   826	        /// Getting return value from dialog
   827	        /// </summary>
   828	        [Test]
   829	        public async Task AsyncDialogShouldReturnTheReturnValue()
   830	        {
   831	            var comp = Context.Render<MudDialogProvider>();
   832	            comp.Markup.Trim().Should().BeEmpty();
   833	            var service = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<IDialogService>();
   834	            service.Should().NotBe(null);
   835	            var dialogReferenceLazy = new Lazy<Task<IDialogReference>>(service.ShowAsync<DialogWithReturnValue>);
   836	            // open dialog
   837	            await comp.InvokeAsync(() => dialogReferenceLazy.Value);
   838	            var dialogReference = await dialogReferenceLazy.Value;
   839	            dialogReference.Should().NotBe(null);
   840	            // close by click on cancel button
   841	            comp.FindAll("button")[0].Click();
   842	            var rv = await dialogReference.GetReturnValueAsync<string>();
   843	            rv.Should().BeNull();
   844	            // open dialog
   845	            dialogReferenceLazy = new Lazy<Task<IDialogReference>>(() => service.ShowAsync<DialogWithReturnValue>());
   846	            await comp.InvokeAsync(() => dialogReferenceLazy.Value);
   847	            // close by click on ok button
   848	            comp.FindAll("button")[1].Click();
   849	            dialogReference = await dialogReferenceLazy.Value;
   850	            rv = await dialogReference.GetReturnValueAsync<string>();
   851	            rv.Should().Be("Closed via OK");
   852	        }

   854	        [Test]
   855	        public async Task AsyncDialogKeyboardNavigation()
   856	        {
   857	            var keyInterceptorService = Context.AddKeyInterceptorService();
   858	            var comp = Context.Render<MudDialogProvider>();
   859	            comp.Markup.Trim().Should().BeEmpty();
   860	            var service = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<IDialogService>();
   861	            service.Should().NotBe(null);
   862	            var dialogReferenceLazy = new Lazy<Task<IDialogReference>>(() => service.ShowAsync<DialogOkCancel>(string.Empty, new DialogOptions() { CloseOnEscapeKey = true }));
   863	            //dialog with clickable backdrop
   864	            await comp.InvokeAsync(() => dialogReferenceLazy.Value);
   865	            dialogReferenceLazy.Value.Should().NotBe(null);
   866	            var dialogReference = await dialogReferenceLazy.Value;
   867	            var dialog1 = (DialogOkCancel)dialogReference.Dialog!;
   868	            var dialogInstance1 = dialog1.MudDialog.GetDialogContainer();
   869	            comp.Markup.Trim().Should().NotBeEmpty();
   870	            await comp.InvokeAsync(() => keyInterceptorService.OnKeyDown(dialogInstance1.ElementId, new KeyboardEventArgs { Key = "Escape", Type = "keydown", }));
   871	            comp.Markup.Trim().Should().BeEmpty();
   872	            //dialog with disabled backdrop click
   873	            dialogReferenceLazy = new Lazy<Task<IDialogReference>>(() => service.ShowAsync<DialogOkCancel>(string.Empty, new DialogOptions() { CloseOnEscapeKey = false }));
   874	            await comp.InvokeAsync(() => dialogReferenceLazy.Value);
   875	            dialogReference = await dialogReferenceLazy.Value;
   876	            dialogReference.Should().NotBe(null);
   877	            var dialog2 = (DialogOkCancel)dialogReference.Dialog!;
   878	            var dialogInstance2 = dialog2.MudDialog.GetDialogContainer();
   879	            comp.Markup.Trim().Should().NotBeEmpty();
   880	            await comp.InvokeAsync(() => keyInterceptorService.OnKeyDown(dialogInstance2.ElementId, new KeyboardEventArgs { Key = "Escape", Type = "keydown", }));
   881	            comp.Markup.Trim().Should().NotBeEmpty();
   882	        }

   973	        [Test]
   974	        public async Task ShowGeneric_ShouldProvideDefaultOptions_WhenOverloadIsCalled()
   975	        {
   976	            // Arrange
   977	            var provider = Context.Render<MudDialogProvider>();
   978	            var service = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<IDialogService>();
   979	
   980	            // Act
   981	            await provider.InvokeAsync(async () => await service.ShowAsync<DialogOkCancel>("Custom title"));
   982	            var dialogInstance = provider.FindComponent<MudDialogContainer>();
   983

  1118	        [Test]
  1119	        public async Task Close_ShouldCloseDialogAndInvokeOnDialogCloseRequested()
  1120	        {
  1121	            // Arrange
  1122	            var provider = Context.Render<MudDialogProvider>();
  1123	            var service = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<IDialogService>();
  1124	            var reference = service.CreateReference();
  1125	            var invoked = false;
  1126	            Type type = null;
  1127	            service.OnDialogCloseRequested += (_, result) =>
  1128	            {
  1129	                invoked = true;
  1130	                type = result.DataType;
  1131	            };
  1132	
  1133	            // Act
  1134	            await provider.InvokeAsync(() => service.Close(reference));
  1135	
  1136	            // Assert
  1137	            invoked.Should().BeTrue();
  1138	            type.Should().BeNull();
  1139	        }

  1239	        [TestCase("", false, "mud-dialog-content")]
  1240	        [TestCase("", true, "mud-dialog-content mud-dialog-no-side-padding")]
  1241	        [TestCase("my-class", false, "mud-dialog-content my-class")]
  1242	        [TestCase("my-class", true, "mud-dialog-content mud-dialog-no-side-padding my-class")]
  1243	        public async Task DialogWithContentClassValueShouldRenderExpectedClassname(string contentClass, bool disablePadding, string expectedClassname)
  1244	        {
  1245	            var comp = Context.Render<MudDialogProvider>();
  1246	            comp.Markup.Trim().Should().BeEmpty();
  1247	            var service = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<IDialogService>();
  1248	            service.Should().NotBeNull();
  1249	            IDialogReference dialogReference = null;
  1250	
  1251	            var parameters = new DialogParameters<DialogWithContentClass>
  1252	            {
  1253	                { x => x.ContentClass, contentClass },
  1254	                { x => x.Gutters, !disablePadding }
  1255	            };
  1256	
  1257	            await comp.InvokeAsync(async () => dialogReference = await service.ShowAsync<DialogWithContentClass>(string.Empty, parameters));
  1258	            dialogReference.Should().NotBeNull();
  1259	
  1260	            comp.Find("div.mud-dialog-content").GetAttribute("class").Should().Be(expectedClassname);
  1261	        }

  1305	        [Test]
  1306	        public async Task DialogWithNestedDialogOptionShouldNotReset()
  1307	        {
  1308	            var comp = Context.Render<MudDialogProvider>();
  1309	            comp.Markup.Trim().Should().BeEmpty();
  1310	            var service = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<IDialogService>();
  1311	
  1312	            var dialogReferenceLazy = new Lazy<Task<IDialogReference>>(() => service.ShowAsync<DialogOptionMutation>("Simple Dialog"));
  1313	            await comp.InvokeAsync(async () => await dialogReferenceLazy.Value);
  1314	            var dialogReference = await dialogReferenceLazy.Value;
  1315	            dialogReference.Should().NotBe(null);
  1316	
  1317	            var closeButton = () => comp.Find("button.mud-button-close");
  1318	            // Close button should be visible
  1319	            closeButton().Should().NotBeNull();
  1320	            var button = comp.Find("#show_messagebox_btn");
  1321	            // Open nested message box
  1322	            button.Click();
  1323	
  1324	            var messageBox = () => comp.Find("div.mud-message-box");
  1325	
  1326	            // close message box by clicking on yes button.
  1327	            messageBox().Should().NotBeNull();
  1328	            comp.FindAll(".mud-dialog-actions button")[1].Click();
  1329	            messageBox.Should().Throw<ElementNotFoundException>();
  1330	
  1331	            // Close button should still be visible
  1332	            closeButton().Should().NotBeNull();
  1333	        }
