  1335	        /// <summary>
  1336	        /// Ensures dialog will only be shown once
  1337	        /// </summary>
  1338	        [Test]
  1339	        public async Task DuplicateDialog()
  1340	        {
  1341	            var comp = Context.Render<MudDialogProvider>();
  1342	            var service = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<IDialogService>();
  1343	            service.Should().NotBe(null);
  1344	            var comp1 = Context.Render<InlineDialogDuplicateTest>();
  1345	            // open the dialog
  1346	            comp1.Find("button").Click();
  1347	            await Task.Delay(1000);
  1348	            comp.FindComponents<MudDialog>().Count.Should().Be(1);
  1349	        }
  1350	
  1351	        [Test]
  1352	        public async Task Dialog_TitleContent_And_ProgressBar_ShouldUpdate()
  1353	        {
  1354	            var comp = Context.Render<MudDialogProvider>();
  1355	            comp.Markup.Trim().Should().BeEmpty();
  1356	
  1357	            var service = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<IDialogService>();
  1358	
  1359	            var comp1 = Context.Render<InlineDialogTitleRefreshTest>();
  1360	
  1361	            comp1.Find("button").Click();
  1362	
  1363	            var dialog = comp.Find("div.mud-dialog");
  1364	
  1365	            dialog.Should().NotBeNull();
  1366	            dialog.TextContent.Trim().Should().Be("Initial state"); // Initial title
  1367	            comp.FindComponent<MudProgressLinear>().Instance.Value.Should().Be(0); // Initial progress value - 0
  1368	
  1369	            // Wait for mid-progress (simulate that loop is progressing)
  1370	            await Task.Delay(1000);
  1371	            //comp.Render();
  1372	
  1373	            dialog = comp.Find("div.mud-dialog");
  1374	            dialog.TextContent.Should().Contain("Progress"); //change from "Initial state" to "Progress"
  1375	
  1376	            var progressComponent = comp.FindComponent<MudProgressLinear>();
  1377	            var progressValue = progressComponent.Instance.Value;
  1378	            progressValue.Should().BeGreaterThan(0).And.BeLessThan(100);
  1379	
  1380	            // Wait for dialog to close and state reset
  1381	            await Task.Delay(3000); // Ensure loop finishes
  1382	            comp.Render();
  1383	
  1384	            // Assert the dialog is now hidden
  1385	            comp.Markup.Trim().Should().NotContain("mud-dialog");
  1386	        }
  1387	
  1388	        [Test]
  1389	        public async Task HandleBackgroundClickAsync_Should_Call_RefocusDialogAsync_When_BackdropClick_Disabled()
  1390	        {
  1391	            // Arrange
  1392	            IDialogReference dialogReference = null;
  1393	
  1394	            var jsRuntimeMock = new Mock<IJSRuntime>();
  1395	
  1396	            jsRuntimeMock.Setup(x => x.InvokeAsync<IJSVoidResult>("Blazor._internal.domWrapper.focus", It.IsAny<object[]>()));
  1397	
  1398	            Context.Services.AddSingleton(jsRuntimeMock.Object);
  1399	
  1400	            var comp = Context.Render<MudDialogProvider>();
  1401	            var service = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<IDialogService>();
  1402	            var dialogOptions = new DialogOptions { BackdropClick = false, CloseOnEscapeKey = true };
  1403	
  1404	            //Act
  1405	            await comp.InvokeAsync(async () => dialogReference = await service.ShowAsync<DialogOkCancel>("Test Dialog True", dialogOptions));
  1406	
  1407	            jsRuntimeMock.Verify(x => x.InvokeAsync<IJSVoidResult>("Blazor._internal.domWrapper.focus",
  1408	                It.Is<object[]>(args =>
  1409	                    args.Length == 2 &&
  1410	                    args[0] is ElementReference &&
  1411	                    args[1] is bool)),
  1412	                Times.AtMost(1)); // Focus should be called once when dialog is opened (FocusTrap)
  1413	
  1414	            comp.Find("div.mud-dialog-container").Should().NotBeNull(); // Dialog is open
  1415	            comp.Find("div.mud-overlay").Click(); // Simulate a click on the backdrop
  1416	
  1417	            //Assert
  1418	            comp.Find("div.mud-dialog-container").Should().NotBeNull(); // Dialog should still be open
  1419	
  1420	            jsRuntimeMock.Verify(x => x.InvokeAsync<IJSVoidResult>("Blazor._internal.domWrapper.focus",
  1421	                It.Is<object[]>(args =>
  1422	                    args.Length == 2 &&
  1423	                    args[0] is ElementReference &&
  1424	                    args[1] is bool)),
  1425	                Times.AtMost(2)); // Focus should be called once when dialog is opened and once when backdrop is clicked (refouus)
  1426	        }
  1427	
  1428	        [Test]
  1429	        public async Task HandleBackgroundClickAsync_Should_Call_OnBackdropClick_And_RefocusDialog_When_Delegate_Defined()
  1430	        {
  1431	            // Arrange
  1432	            var jsRuntimeMock = new Mock<IJSRuntime>();
  1433	
  1434	            jsRuntimeMock.Setup(x => x.InvokeAsync<IJSVoidResult>("Blazor._internal.domWrapper.focus", It.IsAny<object[]>()));
  1435	
  1436	            Context.Services.AddSingleton(jsRuntimeMock.Object);
  1437	
  1438	            var comp = Context.Render<MudDialogProvider>();
  1439	
  1440	            // Create a dialog component with OnBackdropClick delegate
  1441	            var dialogComponent = Context.Render<DialogCloseOnEscapeTest>();
  1442	
  1443	            // Act
  1444	            await dialogComponent.FindComponent<MudButton>().Find("button").ClickAsync(new MouseEventArgs()); // Open the dialog
  1445	
  1446	            jsRuntimeMock.Verify(x => x.InvokeAsync<IJSVoidResult>("Blazor._internal.domWrapper.focus",
  1447	                It.Is<object[]>(args =>
  1448	                    args.Length == 2 &&
  1449	                    args[0] is ElementReference &&
  1450	                    args[1] is bool)),
  1451	                Times.AtMost(1)); // Focus should be called once when dialog is opened
  1452	
  1453	            comp.Find("div.mud-dialog-container").Should().NotBeNull(); // Dialog is open
  1454	
  1455	            await comp.Find("div.mud-overlay").ClickAsync(new MouseEventArgs()); // Simulate a click on the backdrop
  1456	
  1457	            // Assert
  1458	            comp.Find("div.mud-dialog-container").Should().NotBeNull(); // Dialog should still be open (not closed)
  1459	
  1460	            jsRuntimeMock.Verify(x => x.InvokeAsync<IJSVoidResult>("Blazor._internal.domWrapper.focus",
  1461	                It.Is<object[]>(args =>
  1462	                    args.Length == 2 &&
  1463	                    args[0] is ElementReference &&
  1464	                    args[1] is bool)),
  1465	                Times.AtMost(2)); // Focus should be called once when dialog opens and once for refocus after backdrop click
  1466	        }
  1467	
  1468	        /// <summary>
  1469	        /// Test that DefaultFocus property on MudDialogProvider is properly cascaded to dialogs
  1470	        /// </summary>
  1471	        [Test]
  1472	        public async Task DialogProvider_DefaultFocus_ShouldCascadeToDialogs()
  1473	        {
  1474	            // Arrange - Create a DialogProvider with DefaultFocus set to None
  1475	            var comp = Context.Render<MudDialogProvider>(parameters => parameters
  1476	                .Add(p => p.DefaultFocus, DefaultFocus.None));
  1477	            var service = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<IDialogService>();
  1478	
  1479	            // Act - Show a dialog without explicitly setting DefaultFocus
  1480	            IDialogReference dialogReference = null;
  1481	            await comp.InvokeAsync(async () => dialogReference = await service.ShowAsync<DialogWithDefaultFocusTest>());
  1482	
  1483	            // Assert - Verify the dialog is shown
  1484	            dialogReference.Should().NotBeNull();
  1485	            comp.Find("div.mud-dialog-container").Should().NotBeNull();
  1486	
  1487	            // Close the dialog
  1488	            await comp.InvokeAsync(() => service.Close(dialogReference));
  1489	        }
  1490	
  1491	        /// <summary>
  1492	        /// Test that MudDialog respects explicit DefaultFocus parameter over global setting
  1493	        /// </summary>
  1494	        [Test]
  1495	        public async Task Dialog_ExplicitDefaultFocus_ShouldOverrideGlobalSetting()
  1496	        {
  1497	            // Arrange - Create a DialogProvider with DefaultFocus set to None
  1498	            var comp = Context.Render<MudDialogProvider>(parameters => parameters
  1499	                .Add(p => p.DefaultFocus, DefaultFocus.None));
  1500	            var service = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<IDialogService>();
  1501	
  1502	            // Act - Show a dialog WITH explicitly setting DefaultFocus to FirstChild
  1503	            var dialogParameters = new DialogParameters
  1504	            {
  1505	                [nameof(MudDialog.DefaultFocus)] = DefaultFocus.FirstChild
  1506	            };
  1507	            IDialogReference dialogReference = null;
  1508	            await comp.InvokeAsync(async () => dialogReference = await service.ShowAsync<DialogWithDefaultFocusTest>(title: "Test", parameters: dialogParameters));
  1509	
  1510	            // Assert - Verify the dialog is shown
  1511	            dialogReference.Should().NotBeNull();
  1512	            comp.Find("div.mud-dialog-container").Should().NotBeNull();
  1513	
  1514	            // Close the dialog
  1515	            await comp.InvokeAsync(() => service.Close(dialogReference));
  1516	        }
  1517	
  1518	        [Test]
  1519	        public async Task CloseButton_ShouldHavePreventDefaultOnMouseDownAttribute()
  1520	        {
  1521	            // Arrange
  1522	            var comp = Context.Render<MudDialogProvider>();
  1523	            var service = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<IDialogService>();
  1524	
  1525	            // Act
  1526	            await comp.InvokeAsync(async () =>
  1527	                await service.ShowAsync<DialogOkCancel>("Custom title", new DialogOptions { CloseButton = true }));
  1528	
  1529	            // Assert
  1530	            var closeBtn = comp.Find(".mud-button-close");
  1531	            closeBtn.Should().NotBeNull();
  1532	            closeBtn.GetAttribute("blazor:onmousedown:preventdefault").Should().Be("");
  1533	        }

  1600	
  1601	        /// <summary>
  1602	        /// HasRouteChanged() should return true if the dialog reference's options
  1603	        /// CloseOnNavigation is set to null and the absolute path has changed.
  1604	        /// </summary>
  1605	        [Test]
  1606	        public async Task HasRouteChanged_ShouldBeTrueWhenCloseOnNavigationIsNullAndRouteChanged()
  1607	        {
  1608	            var service = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<IDialogService>();
  1609	            var navigationManager = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<NavigationManager>();
  1610	            var provider = Context.Render<MudDialogProvider>();
  1611	
  1612	            var currentRoute = navigationManager.ToAbsoluteUri($"/test/{Guid.NewGuid()}").ToString();
  1613	            navigationManager.NavigateTo(currentRoute);
  1614	
  1615	            var reference = await service.ShowAsync<DialogOkCancel>();
  1616	            var options = new DialogOptions
  1617	            {
  1618	                CloseOnNavigation = null
  1619	            };
  1620	            reference.InjectOptions(options);
  1621	
  1622	            var changedRoute = navigationManager.ToAbsoluteUri($"{currentRoute}/{Guid.NewGuid()}").AbsolutePath.TrimEnd('/');
  1623	            provider.Instance.HasRouteChanged(changedRoute).Should().BeTrue();
  1624	        }
  1625	
  1626	        /// <summary>
  1627	        /// HasRouteChanged() should return false if the dialog reference's options
  1628	        /// CloseOnNavigation is set to null and only the query or fragment has changed.
  1629	        /// </summary>
  1630	        [Test]
  1631	        public async Task HasRouteChanged_ShouldBeFalseWhenCloseOnNavigationIsNullAndQueryOrFragmentChanged()
  1632	        {
  1633	            var service = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<IDialogService>();
  1634	            var navigationManager = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<NavigationManager>();
  1635	            var provider = Context.Render<MudDialogProvider>();
  1636	
  1637	            var currentRoute = navigationManager.ToAbsoluteUri($"/test/{Guid.NewGuid()}").ToString();
  1638	            navigationManager.NavigateTo(currentRoute);
  1639	
  1640	            var reference = await service.ShowAsync<DialogOkCancel>();
  1641	            var options = new DialogOptions
  1642	            {
  1643	                CloseOnNavigation = null
  1644	            };
  1645	            reference.InjectOptions(options);
  1646	
  1647	            var changedQuery = navigationManager.ToAbsoluteUri($"{currentRoute}?query={Guid.NewGuid()}").AbsolutePath;
  1648	            provider.Instance.HasRouteChanged(changedQuery).Should().BeFalse();
  1649	
  1650	            var changedFragment = navigationManager.ToAbsoluteUri($"{currentRoute}#{Guid.NewGuid()}").AbsolutePath;
  1651	            provider.Instance.HasRouteChanged(changedFragment).Should().BeFalse();
  1652	        }
  1653	
  1654	        /// <summary>
  1655	        /// HasRouteChanged() should return true if the dialog reference's options is set to null
  1656	        /// and the absolute path has changed.
  1657	        /// </summary>
  1658	        [Test]
  1659	        public async Task HasRouteChanged_ShouldBeTrueWhenOptionsIsNullAndRouteChanged()
  1660	        {
  1661	            var service = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<IDialogService>();
  1662	            var navigationManager = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<NavigationManager>();
  1663	            var provider = Context.Render<MudDialogProvider>();
  1664	
  1665	            var currentRoute = navigationManager.ToAbsoluteUri($"/test/{Guid.NewGuid()}").ToString();
  1666	            navigationManager.NavigateTo(currentRoute);
  1667	
  1668	            var reference = await service.ShowAsync<DialogOkCancel>();
  1669	            reference.InjectOptions(null);
  1670	
  1671	            var changedRoute = navigationManager.ToAbsoluteUri($"{currentRoute}/{Guid.NewGuid()}").AbsolutePath.TrimEnd('/');
  1672	            provider.Instance.HasRouteChanged(changedRoute).Should().BeTrue();
  1673	        }
  1674	
  1675	        /// <summary>
  1676	        /// HasRouteChanged() should return false if the dialog reference's options is set to null
  1677	        /// and only the query or fragment has changed.
  1678	        /// </summary>
  1679	        [Test]
  1680	        public async Task HasRouteChanged_ShouldBeFalseWhenOptionsIsNullAndQueryOrFragmentChanged()
  1681	        {
  1682	            var service = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<IDialogService>();
  1683	            var navigationManager = Context.Services.GetRequiredService<NavigationManager>();
  1684	            var provider = Context.Render<MudDialogProvider>();
  1685	
  1686	            var currentRoute = navigationManager.ToAbsoluteUri($"/test/{Guid.NewGuid()}").ToString();
  1687	            navigationManager.NavigateTo(currentRoute);
  1688	
  1689	            var reference = await service.ShowAsync<DialogOkCancel>();
  1690	            reference.InjectOptions(null);
  1691	
  1692	            var changedQuery = navigationManager.ToAbsoluteUri($"{currentRoute}?query={Guid.NewGuid()}").AbsolutePath;
  1693	            provider.Instance.HasRouteChanged(changedQuery).Should().BeFalse();
  1694	
  1695	            var changedFragment = navigationManager.ToAbsoluteUri($"{currentRoute}#{Guid.NewGuid()}").AbsolutePath;
  1696	            provider.Instance.HasRouteChanged(changedFragment).Should().BeFalse();
  1697	        }
  1698	    }
  1699	    internal class CustomDialogService : DialogService
  1700	    {
  1701	        public override IDialogReference CreateReference() => new CustomDialogReference(Guid.NewGuid(), this);
  1702	    }
  1703	
  1704	    internal class CustomDialogReference : DialogReference
  1705	    {
  1706	        public bool AllowDismiss { get; set; }
  1707	
  1708	        public CustomDialogReference(Guid dialogInstanceId, IDialogService dialogService) : base(dialogInstanceId, dialogService) { }
  1709	
  1710	        public override bool Dismiss(DialogResult result)
  1711	        {
  1712	            return AllowDismiss;
  1713	        }
  1714	    }
  1715	}

Good, the project builds. Now let me implement the changes. Based on the PR description, I need to add:

1. In `IDialogService.cs`:
   - `Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[T]>(DialogParameters<T> parameters) where T : IComponent;`
   - `Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[T]>(DialogParameters<T> parameters, DialogOptions? options) where T : IComponent;`

2. In `DialogService.cs`:
   - Corresponding implementations

Also, looking at the test at line 346-352 and 959-965, the existing tests use `DialogParameters<DialogWithParameters>` with `string.Empty` as the title. The PR wants to avoid needing the `string.Empty`.

Let me also check if there's a type-level generic `ShowAsync` that takes `DialogParameters<T>` without title. Let me check the existing method at line 84-87 of `DialogService.cs`:

```csharp
public Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers...] T>(string? title, DialogParameters parameters) where T : IComponent
```

This takes non-generic `DialogParameters`. The new overloads should take `DialogParameters<T>`.

Now, let me make the edits:

import re

filepath = '/testbed/src/MudBlazor/Services/Dialog/IDialogService.cs'

with open(filepath, 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

# Find the line with: Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[...]>(string? title, DialogParameters parameters) where TComponent : IComponent;
# Then insert the new overload after it

old = '''        /// <summary>
        /// Displays a dialog with a custom title and parameters.
        /// </summary>
        /// <typeparam name="TComponent">The dialog to display.</typeparam>
        /// <param name="title">The text at the top of the dialog.</param>
        /// <param name="parameters">The custom parameters to set within the dialog.</param>
        /// <returns>A reference to the dialog.</returns>
        Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] TComponent>(string? title, DialogParameters parameters) where TComponent : IComponent;

        /// <summary>
        /// Displays a dialog with a custom title, parameters, and options.
        /// </summary>
        /// <typeparam name="TComponent">The dialog to display.</typeparam>
        /// <param name="title">The text at the top of the dialog.</param>
        /// <param name="parameters">The custom parameters to set within the dialog.</param>
        /// <param name="options">The custom display options for the dialog.</param>
        /// <returns>A reference to the dialog.</returns>
        Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] TComponent>(string? title, DialogParameters parameters, DialogOptions? options) where TComponent : IComponent;'''

new = '''        /// <summary>
        /// Displays a dialog with a custom title and parameters.
        /// </summary>
        /// <typeparam name="TComponent">The dialog to display.</typeparam>
        /// <param name="title">The text at the top of the dialog.</param>
        /// <param name="parameters">The custom parameters to set within the dialog.</param>
        /// <returns>A reference to the dialog.</returns>
        Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] TComponent>(string? title, DialogParameters parameters) where TComponent : IComponent;

        /// <summary>
        /// Displays a dialog with parameters.
        /// </summary>
        /// <typeparam name="TComponent">The dialog to display.</typeparam>
        /// <param name="parameters">The custom parameters to set within the dialog.</param>
        /// <returns>A reference to the dialog.</returns>
        Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] TComponent>(DialogParameters<TComponent> parameters) where TComponent : IComponent;

        /// <summary>
        /// Displays a dialog with a custom title, parameters, and options.
        /// </summary>
        /// <typeparam name="TComponent">The dialog to display.</typeparam>
        /// <param name="title">The text at the top of the dialog.</param>
        /// <param name="parameters">The custom parameters to set within the dialog.</param>
        /// <param name="options">The custom display options for the dialog.</param>
        /// <returns>A reference to the dialog.</returns>
        Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] TComponent>(string? title, DialogParameters parameters, DialogOptions? options) where TComponent : IComponent;

        /// <summary>
        /// Displays a dialog with parameters and options.
        /// </summary>
        /// <typeparam name="TComponent">The dialog to display.</typeparam>
        /// <param name="parameters">The custom parameters to set within the dialog.</param>
        /// <param name="options">The custom display options for the dialog.</param>
        /// <returns>A reference to the dialog.</returns>
        Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] TComponent>(DialogParameters<TComponent> parameters, DialogOptions? options) where TComponent : IComponent;'''

if old in content:
    content = content.replace(old, new)
    with open(filepath, 'w') as f:
        f.write(content)
    print("SUCCESS: IDialogService.cs updated")
else:
    print("FAILED: Could not find the old string in IDialogService.cs")
    # debug: find where the mismatch is
    print("Looking for old string...")
    print(repr(old))
EOF

    10	namespace MudBlazor
    11	{
    12	    /// <summary>
    13	    /// A service for managing <see cref="MudDialog"/> components.
    14	    /// </summary>
    15	    /// <remarks>
    16	    /// This service requires a <see cref="MudDialogProvider"/> in your layout page.
    17	    /// </remarks>
    18	    public interface IDialogService
    19	    {
    20	
    21	        /// <summary>
    22	        /// Occurs when a new dialog instance is created.
    23	        /// </summary>
    24	        event Func<IDialogReference, Task> DialogInstanceAddedAsync;
    25	
    26	        /// <summary>
    27	        /// Occurs when a request is made to close a dialog.
    28	        /// </summary>
    29	        event Action<IDialogReference, DialogResult?>? OnDialogCloseRequested;
    30	
    31	        /// <summary>
    32	        /// Displays a dialog.
    33	        /// </summary>
    34	        /// <typeparam name="TComponent">The dialog to display.</typeparam>
    35	        /// <returns>A reference to the dialog.</returns>
    36	        Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] TComponent>() where TComponent : IComponent;
    37	
    38	        /// <summary>
    39	        /// Displays a dialog with a custom title.
    40	        /// </summary>
    41	        /// <typeparam name="TComponent">The dialog to display.</typeparam>
    42	        /// <param name="title">The text at the top of the dialog.</param>
    43	        /// <returns>A reference to the dialog.</returns>
    44	        Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] TComponent>(string? title) where TComponent : IComponent;
    45	
    46	        /// <summary>
    47	        /// Displays a dialog with a custom title and options.
    48	        /// </summary>
    49	        /// <typeparam name="TComponent">The dialog to display.</typeparam>
    50	        /// <param name="title">The text at the top of the dialog.</param>
    51	        /// <param name="options">The custom display options for the dialog.</param>
    52	        /// <returns>A reference to the dialog.</returns>
    53	        Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] TComponent>(string? title, DialogOptions options) where TComponent : IComponent;
    54

    60	        /// <param name="parameters">The custom parameters to set within the dialog.</param>
    61	        /// <returns>A reference to the dialog.</returns>
    62	        Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] TComponent>(string? title, DialogParameters parameters) where TComponent : IComponent;
    63

    65	        /// Displays a dialog with parameters.

    70	        Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] TComponent>(DialogParameters<TComponent> parameters) where TComponent : IComponent;
    71

    74	        /// </summary>

   132	        /// Shows a simple dialog with a title, message, and up to three custom buttons.
   133	        /// </summary>
   134	        /// <param name="title">The text at the top of the dialog.</param>
   135	        /// <param name="message">The message displayed under the title.</param>
   136	        /// <param name="yesText">The text of the "Yes" button.  Defaults to <c>OK</c>.</param>

   138	        /// <param name="cancelText">The text of the "Cancel" button.  Defaults to <c>null</c>.</param>
   139	        /// <param name="options">The custom display options for the dialog.  Defaults to <c>null</c>.</param>
   140	        /// <returns>Returns <c>null</c> if the <c>Cancel</c> button was clicked, <c>true</c> if the <c>Yes</c> button was clicked, or <c>false</c> if the <c>No</c> button was clicked.</returns>
   141	        Task<bool?> ShowMessageBoxAsync(string? title, string message, string yesText = "OK",
   142	            string? noText = null, string? cancelText = null, DialogOptions? options = null);
   143	
   144	        /// <summary>
   145	        /// Shows a simple dialog with a title, HTML message, and up to three custom buttons.
   146	        /// </summary>
   147	        /// <param name="title">The text at the top of the dialog.</param>
   148	        /// <param name="markupMessage">The HTML message displayed under the title.</param>
   149	        /// <param name="yesText">The text of the "Yes" button.  Defaults to <c>OK</c>.</param>
   150	        /// <param name="noText">The text of the "No" button.  Defaults to <c>null</c>.</param>
   151	        /// <param name="cancelText">The text of the "Cancel" button.  Defaults to <c>null</c>.</param>
   152	        /// <param name="options">The custom display options for the dialog.  Defaults to <c>null</c>.</param>
   153	        /// <returns>Returns <c>null</c> if the <c>Cancel</c> button was clicked, <c>true</c> if the <c>Yes</c> button was clicked, or <c>false</c> if the <c>No</c> button was clicked.</returns>
   154	        Task<bool?> ShowMessageBoxAsync(string? title, MarkupString markupMessage, string yesText = "OK",
   155	            string? noText = null, string? cancelText = null, DialogOptions? options = null);
   156

   163	        Task<bool?> ShowMessageBoxAsync(MessageBoxOptions messageBoxOptions, DialogOptions? options = null);

OBSERVATION:

     1	﻿// Copyright (c) 2019 Blazored (https://github.com/Blazored)
     2	// See https://github.com/Blazored
     3	// License: MIT
     4	// Copyright (c) 2020 Adapted by MudBlazor
     5	
     6	using System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis;
     7	using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components;
     8	
     9	namespace MudBlazor
    10	{
    11	    /// <summary>
    12	    /// Service used to create, show, and close MudBlazor dialogs.
    13	    /// </summary>
    14	    /// <remarks>
    15	    /// Register this service and include a <see cref="MudDialogProvider"/> in your layout so dialogs can render and respond to close requests.
    16	    /// </remarks>
    17	    /// <seealso cref="MudDialog"/>
    18	    /// <seealso cref="MudDialogContainer"/>
    19	    /// <seealso cref="MudDialogProvider"/>
    20	    /// <seealso cref="DialogOptions"/>
    21	    /// <seealso cref="DialogParameters{T}"/>
    22	    /// <seealso cref="DialogReference"/>
    23	    public class DialogService : IDialogService
    24	    {
    25	        /// <summary>
    26	        /// Internal wrapper component that prevents overwriting parameters on existing dialog instances.
    27	        /// </summary>
    28	        /// <remarks>
    29	        /// This keeps dialog content stable while the parent fragment re-renders.
    30	        /// See: https://github.com/MudBlazor/MudBlazor/issues/10659#issuecomment-2602911059
    31	        /// </remarks>
    32	        private sealed class DialogHelperComponent : IComponent
    33	        {
    34	            private const string ChildContent = nameof(ChildContent);
    35	            private RenderFragment? _renderFragment;
    36	            private RenderHandle _renderHandle;
    37	            void IComponent.Attach(RenderHandle renderHandle) => _renderHandle = renderHandle;
    38	
    39	            Task IComponent.SetParametersAsync(ParameterView parameters)
    40	            {
    41	                if (_renderFragment is null && parameters.TryGetValue<RenderFragment>(ChildContent, out var renderFragment))
    42	                {
    43	                    _renderFragment = renderFragment;
    44	                    _renderHandle.Render(_renderFragment);
    45	                }
    46	
    47	                return Task.CompletedTask;
    48	            }
    49	
    50	            public static RenderFragment Wrap(RenderFragment renderFragment)
    51	                => builder =>
    52	                {
    53	                    builder.OpenComponent<DialogHelperComponent>(1);
    54	                    builder.AddAttribute(2, ChildContent, renderFragment);
    55	                    builder.CloseComponent();
    56	                };
    57	        }
    58	
    59	        /// <inheritdoc />
    60	        public event Func<IDialogReference, Task>? DialogInstanceAddedAsync;
    61	
    62	        /// <inheritdoc />
    63	        public event Action<IDialogReference, DialogResult?>? OnDialogCloseRequested;
    64	
    65	        /// <inheritdoc />
    66	        public Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] T>() where T : IComponent
    67	        {
    68	            return ShowAsync<T>(string.Empty, DialogParameters.Default, DialogOptions.Default);
    69	        }
    70	
    71	        /// <inheritdoc />
    72	        public Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] T>(string? title) where T : IComponent
    73	        {
    74	            return ShowAsync<T>(title, DialogParameters.Default, DialogOptions.Default);
    75	        }
    76	
    77	        /// <inheritdoc />
    78	        public Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] T>(string? title, DialogOptions options) where T : IComponent
    79	        {
    80	            return ShowAsync<T>(title, DialogParameters.Default, options);
    81	        }
    82	
    83	        /// <inheritdoc />
    84	        public Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] T>(string? title, DialogParameters parameters) where T : IComponent
    85	        {
    86	            return ShowAsync<T>(title, parameters, DialogOptions.Default);
    87	        }
    88	
    89	        /// <inheritdoc />
    90	        public Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] T>(string? title, DialogParameters parameters,
    91	            DialogOptions? options) where T : IComponent
    92	        {
    93	            return ShowAsync(typeof(T), title, parameters, options ?? DialogOptions.Default);
    94	        }
    95	
    96	        /// <inheritdoc />
    97	        public Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync([DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] Type component)
    98	        {
    99	            return ShowAsync(component, string.Empty, DialogParameters.Default, DialogOptions.Default);
   100	        }
   101	
   102	        /// <inheritdoc />
   103	        public Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync([DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] Type component, string? title)
   104	        {
   105	            return ShowAsync(component, title, DialogParameters.Default, DialogOptions.Default);
   106	        }
   107	
   108	        /// <inheritdoc />
   109	        public Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync([DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] Type component, string? title, DialogOptions options)
   110	        {
   111	            return ShowAsync(component, title, DialogParameters.Default, options);
   112	        }
   113	
   114	        /// <inheritdoc />
   115	        public Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync([DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] Type component, string? title, DialogParameters parameters)
   116	        {
   117	            return ShowAsync(component, title, parameters, DialogOptions.Default);
   118	        }
   119	
   120	        /// <inheritdoc />
   121	        public async Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync([DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] Type component, string? title,
   122	            DialogParameters parameters, DialogOptions options)
   123	        {
   124	            var dialogReference = await ShowCoreAsync(component, title, parameters, options);
   125	
   126	            //Do not wait forever, what if render fails because of some internal exception and we will never release the method.
   127	            var cancellationTokenSource = new CancellationTokenSource(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(5));
   128	            var token = cancellationTokenSource.Token;
   129	            await using (token.Register(() => dialogReference.RenderCompleteTaskCompletionSource.TrySetResult(false)))
   130	            {
   131	                await dialogReference.RenderCompleteTaskCompletionSource.Task;
   132	
   133	                return dialogReference;
   134	            }
   135	        }
   136	
   137	        /// <inheritdoc />
   138	        public Task<bool?> ShowMessageBoxAsync(string? title, string message, string yesText = "OK",
   139	            string? noText = null, string? cancelText = null, DialogOptions? options = null)
   140	        {
   141	            return ShowMessageBoxAsync(new MessageBoxOptions
   142	            {
   143	                Title = title,
   144	                Message = message,
   145	                YesText = yesText,
   146	                NoText = noText,
   147	                CancelText = cancelText,
   148	            }, options);
   149	        }
   150	
   151	        /// <inheritdoc />
   152	        public Task<bool?> ShowMessageBoxAsync(string? title, MarkupString markupMessage, string yesText = "OK",
   153	            string? noText = null, string? cancelText = null, DialogOptions? options = null)
   154	        {
   155	            return ShowMessageBoxAsync(new MessageBoxOptions
   156	            {
   157	                Title = title,
   158	                MarkupMessage = markupMessage,
   159	                YesText = yesText,
   160	                NoText = noText,
   161	                CancelText = cancelText,
   162	            }, options);
   163	        }
   164	
   165	        /// <inheritdoc />
   166	        public async Task<bool?> ShowMessageBoxAsync(MessageBoxOptions messageBoxOptions, DialogOptions? options = null)
   167	        {
   168	            var parameters = new DialogParameters
   169	            {
   170	                [nameof(MessageBoxOptions.Title)] = messageBoxOptions.Title,
   171	                [nameof(MessageBoxOptions.Message)] = messageBoxOptions.Message,
   172	                [nameof(MessageBoxOptions.MarkupMessage)] = messageBoxOptions.MarkupMessage,
   173	                [nameof(MessageBoxOptions.CancelText)] = messageBoxOptions.CancelText,
   174	                [nameof(MessageBoxOptions.NoText)] = messageBoxOptions.NoText,
   175	                [nameof(MessageBoxOptions.YesText)] = messageBoxOptions.YesText,
   176	            };
   177	            var reference = await ShowAsync<MudMessageBox>(title: messageBoxOptions.Title, parameters: parameters, options: options);
   178	            var result = await reference.Result;
   179	
   180	            if (result is null)
   181	            {
   182	                return null;
   183	            }
   184	
   185	            if (result.Canceled || result.Data is not bool data)
   186	            {
   187	                return null;
   188	            }
   189	
   190	            return data;
   191	        }
   192	
   193	        /// <inheritdoc />
   194	        public void Close(IDialogReference dialog)
   195	        {
   196	            Close(dialog, DialogResult.Ok<object?>(null));
   197	        }
   198	
   199	        /// <inheritdoc />
   200	        public virtual void Close(IDialogReference dialog, DialogResult? result)
   201	        {
   202	            OnDialogCloseRequested?.Invoke(dialog, result);
   203	        }
   204	
   205	        /// <inheritdoc />
   206	        public virtual IDialogReference CreateReference()
   207	        {
   208	            return new DialogReference(Guid.NewGuid(), this);
   209	        }
   210	
   211	        private async Task<IDialogReference> ShowCoreAsync([DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] Type contentComponent, string? title, DialogParameters parameters,
   212	            DialogOptions options)
   213	        {
   214	            if (!typeof(IComponent).IsAssignableFrom(contentComponent))
   215	            {
   216	                throw new ArgumentException($"{contentComponent.FullName} must be a Blazor IComponent");
   217	            }
   218	
   219	            var dialogReference = CreateReference();
   220	            dialogReference.InjectOptions(options);
   221	            var dialogContent = DialogHelperComponent.Wrap(builder =>
   222	            {
   223	                var i = 0;
   224	                builder.OpenComponent(i++, contentComponent);
   225	                foreach (var parameter in parameters)
   226	                {
   227	                    builder.AddAttribute(i++, parameter.Key, parameter.Value);
   228	                }
   229	
   230	                builder.AddComponentReferenceCapture(i, inst => { dialogReference.InjectDialog(inst); });
   231	                builder.CloseComponent();
   232	            });
   233	            var dialogInstance = new RenderFragment(builder =>
   234	            {
   235	                builder.OpenComponent<MudDialogContainer>(0);
   236	                builder.SetKey(dialogReference.Id);
   237	                builder.AddComponentParameter(1, nameof(MudDialogContainer.Options), options);
   238	                builder.AddComponentParameter(2, nameof(MudDialogContainer.Title), title);
   239	                builder.AddComponentParameter(3, nameof(MudDialogContainer.Content), dialogContent);
   240	                builder.AddComponentParameter(4, nameof(MudDialogContainer.Id), dialogReference.Id);
   241	                builder.CloseComponent();
   242	            });
   243	            dialogReference.InjectRenderFragment(dialogInstance);
   244	
   245	            var dialogInstanceAddedAsync = DialogInstanceAddedAsync;
   246	            if (dialogInstanceAddedAsync is not null)
   247	            {
   248	                await dialogInstanceAddedAsync(dialogReference);
   249	            }
   250	
   251	            return dialogReference;
   252	        }
   253	    }
   254	}

     6	using System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis;
     7	using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components;

     9	namespace MudBlazor
    10	{
    11	    /// <summary>
    12	    /// Service used to create, show, and close MudBlazor dialogs.
    13	    /// </summary>
    14	    /// <remarks>
    15	    /// Register this service and include a <see cref="MudDialogProvider"/> in your layout so dialogs can render and respond to close requests.
    16	    /// </remarks>
    17	    /// <seealso cref="MudDialog"/>
    18	    /// <seealso cref="MudDialogContainer"/>
    19	    /// <seealso cref="MudDialogProvider"/>
    20	    /// <seealso cref="DialogOptions"/>
    21	    /// <seealso cref="DialogParameters{T}"/>
    22	    /// <seealso cref="DialogReference"/>
    23	    public class DialogService : IDialogService
    24	    {
    25	        /// <summary>
    26	        /// Internal wrapper component that prevents overwriting parameters on existing dialog instances.
    27	        /// </summary>
    28	        /// <remarks>
    29	        /// This keeps dialog content stable while the parent fragment re-renders.
    30	        /// See: https://github.com/MudBlazor/MudBlazor/issues/10659#issuecomment-2602911059
    31	        /// </remarks>
    32	        private sealed class DialogHelperComponent : IComponent
    33	        {
    34	            private const string ChildContent = nameof(ChildContent);
    35	            private RenderFragment? _renderFragment;
    36	            private RenderHandle _renderHandle;
    37	            void IComponent.Attach(RenderHandle renderHandle) => _renderHandle = renderHandle;
    38	
    39	            Task IComponent.SetParametersAsync(ParameterView parameters)
    40	            {
    41	                if (_renderFragment is null && parameters.TryGetValue<RenderFragment>(ChildContent, out var renderFragment))
    42	                {
    43	                    _renderFragment = renderFragment;
    44	                    _renderHandle.Render(_renderFragment);
    45	                }
    46	
    47	                return Task.CompletedTask;
    48	            }
    49	
    50	            public static RenderFragment Wrap(RenderFragment renderFragment)
    51	                => builder =>
    52	                {
    53	                    builder.OpenComponent<DialogHelperComponent>(1);
    54	                    builder.AddAttribute(2, ChildContent, renderFragment);
    55	                    builder.CloseComponent();
    56	                };
    57	        }
    58	
    59	        /// <inheritdoc />
    60	        public event Func<IDialogReference, Task>? DialogInstanceAddedAsync;
    61	
    62	        /// <inheritdoc />
    63	        public event Action<IDialogReference, DialogResult?>? OnDialogCloseRequested;
    64	
    65	        /// <inheritdoc />
    66	        public Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] T>() where T : IComponent
    67	        {
    68	            return ShowAsync<T>(string.Empty, DialogParameters.Default, DialogOptions.Default);
    69	        }
    70	
    71	        /// <inheritdoc />
    72	        public Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] T>(string? title) where T : IComponent
    73	        {
    74	            return ShowAsync<T>(title, DialogParameters.Default, DialogOptions.Default);
    75	        }
    76	
    77	        /// <inheritdoc />
    78	        public Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] T>(string? title, DialogOptions options) where T : IComponent
    79	        {
    80	            return ShowAsync<T>(title, DialogParameters.Default, options);
    81	        }
    82	
    83	        /// <inheritdoc />
    84	        public Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] T>(string? title, DialogParameters parameters) where T : IComponent
    85	        {
    86	            return ShowAsync<T>(title, parameters, DialogOptions.Default);
    87	        }
    88

    90	        public Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] T>(DialogParameters<T> parameters) where T : IComponent
    91	        {
    92	            return ShowAsync<T>(string.Empty, parameters, DialogOptions.Default);
    93	        }
    94	
    95	        /// <inheritdoc />
    96	        public Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] T>(DialogParameters<T> parameters,
    97	            DialogOptions? options) where T : IComponent
    98	        {
    99	            return ShowAsync<T>(string.Empty, parameters, options ?? DialogOptions.Default);
   100	        }
   101	
   102	        /// <inheritdoc />
   103	        public Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] T>(string? title, DialogParameters parameters,
   104	            DialogOptions? options) where T : IComponent
   105	        {
   106	            return ShowAsync(typeof(T), title, parameters, options ?? DialogOptions.Default);
   107	        }
   108

   110	        public Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync([DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] Type component)
   111	        {
   112	            return ShowAsync(component, string.Empty, DialogParameters.Default, DialogOptions.Default);
   113	        }
   114	
   115	        /// <inheritdoc />
   116	        public Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync([DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] Type component, string? title)
   117	        {
   118	            return ShowAsync(component, title, DialogParameters.Default, DialogOptions.Default);
   119	        }
   120	
   121	        /// <inheritdoc />
   122	        public Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync([DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] Type component, string? title, DialogOptions options)
   123	        {
   124	            return ShowAsync(component, title, DialogParameters.Default, options);
   125	        }
   126	
   127	        /// <inheritdoc />
   128	        public Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync([DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] Type component, string? title, DialogParameters parameters)
   129	        {
   130	            return ShowAsync(component, title, parameters, DialogOptions.Default);
   131	        }
   132	
   133	        /// <inheritdoc />
   134	        public async Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync([DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] Type component, string? title,
   135	            DialogParameters parameters, DialogOptions options)
   136	        {
   137	            var dialogReference = await ShowCoreAsync(component, title, parameters, options);
   138	
   139	            //Do not wait forever, what if render fails because of some internal exception and we will never release the method.
   140	            var cancellationTokenSource = new CancellationTokenSource(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(5));
   141	            var token = cancellationTokenSource.Token;
   142	            await using (token.Register(() => dialogReference.RenderCompleteTaskCompletionSource.TrySetResult(false)))
   143	            {
   144	                await dialogReference.RenderCompleteTaskCompletionSource.Task;
   145	
   146	                return dialogReference;
   147	            }
   148	        }
   149	
   150	        /// <inheritdoc />
   151	        public Task<bool?> ShowMessageBoxAsync(string? title, string message, string yesText = "OK",
   152	            string? noText = null, string? cancelText = null, DialogOptions? options = null)
   153	        {
   154	            return ShowMessageBoxAsync(new MessageBoxOptions
   155	            {
   156	                Title = title,
   157	                Message = message,
   158	                YesText = yesText,
   159	                NoText = noText,
   160	                CancelText = cancelText,
   161	            }, options);
   162	        }
   163	
   164	        /// <inheritdoc />
   165	        public Task<bool?> ShowMessageBoxAsync(string? title, MarkupString markupMessage, string yesText = "OK",
   166	            string? noText = null, string? cancelText = null, DialogOptions? options = null)
   167	        {
   168	            return ShowMessageBoxAsync(new MessageBoxOptions
   169	            {
   170	                Title = title,
   171	                MarkupMessage = markupMessage,
   172	                YesText = yesText,
   173	                NoText = noText,
   174	                CancelText = cancelText,
   175	            }, options);
   176	        }
   177	
   178	        /// <inheritdoc />
   179	        public async Task<bool?> ShowMessageBoxAsync(MessageBoxOptions messageBoxOptions, DialogOptions? options = null)
   180	        {
   181	            var parameters = new DialogParameters
   182	            {
   183	                [nameof(MessageBoxOptions.Title)] = messageBoxOptions.Title,
   184	                [nameof(MessageBoxOptions.Message)] = messageBoxOptions.Message,
   185	                [nameof(MessageBoxOptions.MarkupMessage)] = messageBoxOptions.MarkupMessage,
   186	                [nameof(MessageBoxOptions.CancelText)] = messageBoxOptions.CancelText,
   187	                [nameof(MessageBoxOptions.NoText)] = messageBoxOptions.NoText,
   188	                [nameof(MessageBoxOptions.YesText)] = messageBoxOptions.YesText,
   189	            };
   190	            var reference = await ShowAsync<MudMessageBox>(title: messageBoxOptions.Title, parameters: parameters, options: options);
   191	            var result = await reference.Result;
   192	
   193	            if (result is null)
   194	            {
   195	                return null;
   196	            }
   197	
   198	            if (result.Canceled || result.Data is not bool data)
   199	            {
   200	                return null;
   201	            }
   202	
   203	            return data;
   204	        }
   205	
   206	        /// <inheritdoc />
   207	        public void Close(IDialogReference dialog)
   208	        {
   209	            Close(dialog, DialogResult.Ok<object?>(null));
   210	        }
   211	
   212	        /// <inheritdoc />
   213	        public virtual void Close(IDialogReference dialog, DialogResult? result)
   214	        {
   215	            OnDialogCloseRequested?.Invoke(dialog, result);
   216	        }
   217	
   218	        /// <inheritdoc />
   219	        public virtual IDialogReference CreateReference()
   220	        {
   221	            return new DialogReference(Guid.NewGuid(), this);
   222	        }
   223	
   224	        private async Task<IDialogReference> ShowCoreAsync([DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] Type contentComponent, string? title, DialogParameters parameters,
   225	            DialogOptions options)
   226	        {
   227	            if (!typeof(IComponent).IsAssignableFrom(contentComponent))
   228	            {
   229	                throw new ArgumentException($"{contentComponent.FullName} must be a Blazor IComponent");
   230	            }
   231	
   232	            var dialogReference = CreateReference();
   233	            dialogReference.InjectOptions(options);
   234	            var dialogContent = DialogHelperComponent.Wrap(builder =>
   235	            {
   236	                var i = 0;
   237	                builder.OpenComponent(i++, contentComponent);
   238	                foreach (var parameter in parameters)
   239	                {
   240	                    builder.AddAttribute(i++, parameter.Key, parameter.Value);
   241	                }
   242	
   243	                builder.AddComponentReferenceCapture(i, inst => { dialogReference.InjectDialog(inst); });
   244	                builder.CloseComponent();
   245	            });
   246	            var dialogInstance = new RenderFragment(builder =>
   247	            {
   248	                builder.OpenComponent<MudDialogContainer>(0);
   249	                builder.SetKey(dialogReference.Id);
   250	                builder.AddComponentParameter(1, nameof(MudDialogContainer.Options), options);
   251	                builder.AddComponentParameter(2, nameof(MudDialogContainer.Title), title);
   252	                builder.AddComponentParameter(3, nameof(MudDialogContainer.Content), dialogContent);
   253	                builder.AddComponentParameter(4, nameof(MudDialogContainer.Id), dialogReference.Id);
   254	                builder.CloseComponent();
   255	            });
   256	            dialogReference.InjectRenderFragment(dialogInstance);
   257	
   258	            var dialogInstanceAddedAsync = DialogInstanceAddedAsync;
   259	            if (dialogInstanceAddedAsync is not null)
   260	            {
   261	                await dialogInstanceAddedAsync(dialogReference);
   262	            }
   263	
   264	            return dialogReference;
   265	        }
   266	    }
   267	}

2026-05-29 12:16:56,364 - INFO - swea-agent-MudBlazor__MudBlazor-12908 - 💭 THOUGHT
All 72 dialog tests pass. Now let me think about edge cases:

1. **The new overloads use `DialogParameters<T>` which inherits from `DialogParameters`**. When the new `ShowAsync<T>(DialogParameters<T> parameters)` is called, it delegates to `ShowAsync<T>(string.Empty, parameters, DialogOptions.Default)`. The `parameters` argument is of type `DialogParameters<T>`, which is a subclass of `DialogParameters`, so it can be passed to the `(string? title, DialogParameters parameters, DialogOptions? options)` overload. This is fine.

2. **Ambiguity**: Let's think about potential method resolution ambiguity. The existing overloads are:
   - `ShowAsync<T>(string? title, DialogParameters parameters)` - takes `DialogParameters`
   - The new overload `ShowAsync<T>(DialogParameters<T> parameters)` - takes `DialogParameters<T>`

   If someone calls `ShowAsync<MyDialog>(new DialogParameters<MyDialog> { ... })`, the compiler should prefer the more specific `DialogParameters<T>` overload over the `DialogParameters` overload (since `DialogParameters<T>` is more specific). But wait, there's no ambiguity because the old overload requires `string? title` as the first parameter while the new one takes only `DialogParameters<T>`. So they have different number of parameters, no ambiguity.

3. **What about `ShowAsync<T>(DialogParameters<T> parameters, DialogOptions? options)` vs `ShowAsync<T>(string? title, DialogParameters parameters, DialogOptions? options)`**: These have different first parameter types - `DialogParameters<T>` vs `string? title`. No ambiguity.

4. **The PR description shows wanting to call `await dialogService.ShowAsync<MyDialog>(new DialogParameters<MyDialog>() { { x => x.MyParam, "value" } });`** - This now works with our new overload.

Let me also verify that the extension method scenario from the PR works properly. The PR mentions:
```csharp
public static async Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<T>(this IDialogService dialogService, DialogParameters<T> parameters) where T : IComponent
{
    return await dialogService.ShowAsync<T>("", parameters);
}
```

Our implementation does exactly this: it calls `ShowAsync<T>(string.Empty, parameters, DialogOptions.Default)`.

Now, let me also check if there's a `CustomDialogService` that overrides `CreateReference` - we saw it at the bottom of the DialogTests.cs. It extends `DialogService` and overrides `CreateReference`. Since our new methods are implemented at the `DialogService` level and delegate to the existing `ShowAsync<T>(string, DialogParameters, DialogOptions?)`, the `CustomDialogService` should work fine without changes.

2026-05-29 12:17:30,646 - INFO - swea-agent-MudBlazor__MudBlazor-12908 - 💭 THOUGHT
My changes are:

**IDialogService.cs** (lines 70 and 89):
- `ShowAsync<TComponent>(DialogParameters<TComponent> parameters)` 
- `ShowAsync<TComponent>(DialogParameters<TComponent> parameters, DialogOptions? options)`

**DialogService.cs** (lines 90 and 96):
- `ShowAsync<T>(DialogParameters<T> parameters)` → delegates to `ShowAsync<T>(string.Empty, parameters, DialogOptions.Default)`
- `ShowAsync<T>(DialogParameters<T> parameters, DialogOptions? options)` → delegates to `ShowAsync<T>(string.Empty, parameters, options ?? DialogOptions.Default)`

This matches the PR requirement perfectly. The user can now call:
- `await dialogService.ShowAsync<MyDialog>(new DialogParameters<MyDialog>() { { x => x.MyParam, "value" } });`
- `await dialogService.ShowAsync<MyDialog>(new DialogParameters<MyDialog>() { { x => x.MyParam, "value" } }, new DialogOptions { ... });`

Instead of the previous:
- `await dialogService.ShowAsync<MyDialog>("", new DialogParameters<MyDialog>() { { x => x.MyParam, "value" } });`

    14	    /// </summary>

    17	    /// </remarks>

    19	    {
    20	
    21	        /// <summary>
    22	        /// Occurs when a new dialog instance is created.
    23	        /// </summary>
    24	        event Func<IDialogReference, Task> DialogInstanceAddedAsync;
    25	
    26	        /// <summary>
    27	        /// Occurs when a request is made to close a dialog.
    28	        /// </summary>
    29	        event Action<IDialogReference, DialogResult?>? OnDialogCloseRequested;
    30	
    31	        /// <summary>
    32	        /// Displays a dialog.
    33	        /// </summary>
    34	        /// <typeparam name="TComponent">The dialog to display.</typeparam>
    35	        /// <returns>A reference to the dialog.</returns>
    36	        Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] TComponent>() where TComponent : IComponent;
    37	
    38	        /// <summary>
    39	        /// Displays a dialog with a custom title.
    40	        /// </summary>
    41	        /// <typeparam name="TComponent">The dialog to display.</typeparam>
    42	        /// <param name="title">The text at the top of the dialog.</param>
    43	        /// <returns>A reference to the dialog.</returns>
    44	        Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] TComponent>(string? title) where TComponent : IComponent;
    45	
    46	        /// <summary>
    47	        /// Displays a dialog with a custom title and options.
    48	        /// </summary>
    49	        /// <typeparam name="TComponent">The dialog to display.</typeparam>
    50	        /// <param name="title">The text at the top of the dialog.</param>
    51	        /// <param name="options">The custom display options for the dialog.</param>
    52	        /// <returns>A reference to the dialog.</returns>
    53	        Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] TComponent>(string? title, DialogOptions options) where TComponent : IComponent;
    54	
    55	        /// <summary>
    56	        /// Displays a dialog with a custom title and parameters.
    57	        /// </summary>
    58	        /// <typeparam name="TComponent">The dialog to display.</typeparam>
    59	        /// <param name="title">The text at the top of the dialog.</param>
    60	        /// <param name="parameters">The custom parameters to set within the dialog.</param>
    61	        /// <returns>A reference to the dialog.</returns>
    62	        Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] TComponent>(string? title, DialogParameters parameters) where TComponent : IComponent;

    65	        /// Displays a dialog with parameters.
    66	        /// </summary>
    67	        /// <typeparam name="TComponent">The dialog to display.</typeparam>

    69	        /// <returns>A reference to the dialog.</returns>
    70	        Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] TComponent>(DialogParameters<TComponent> parameters) where TComponent : IComponent;
    71	
    72	        /// <summary>
    73	        /// Displays a dialog with a custom title, parameters, and options.
    74	        /// </summary>
    75	        /// <typeparam name="TComponent">The dialog to display.</typeparam>
